location: Montclair Main - Tower Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
Drop in during this weekly open session for technology related help on using your cellphone, tablet, or computer.


Want to feel more confident using technology? Join us for Tech Help Tuesdays—a casual, drop-in space where adults can receive one-on-one assistance with everyday tech challenges. Come prepared with questions relating to your personal devices!

Registration is required as only one staff member is available to assist at this time. 20-25 minutes are allotted per patron. If registrations are full, please check a future date, as we cannot accommodate walk-ins at this time.

Please note that some technological issues may be above the ability of the library staff and you may be referred out to a better suited tech professional. These drop-ins are not professional IT services, we cannot fix computers or smartphones.

title: Advanced Woodworking
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

For those with previous woodworking experience, this class will allow students to learn more advanced techniques. Students will have the opportunity to start a new project or bring in a project from home that they would like to work on.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

title: Open Book / Open Mind, Nicholas Lemann, "Returning: A Search for Home Across Three Centuries"
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This program is full. But please come to the auditorium at 5:45 p.m. and we'll try to fit you in. Waiting list guests will be admitted at 6:35 p.m. 

Nicholas Lemann, former dean of Columbia Journalism School and staff writer for The New Yorker, will talk to journalist and author Dale Russakoff  ("The Prize") about about his new starkly honest family history, "Returning," in which he traces his Jewish Southern ancestors from Louisiana to Germany and explores their stories from peddlars to plantation owners (and enslavers) as well as shopkeepers and professionals. "Returning" is also a memoir of coming to terms with identity, assimilation and religious observance. Co-presented by Bnai Keshet, Congregation Shomrei Emunah, Temple Ner Tamid and Temple Sholom of West Essex.

"What does it mean to belong? 'Returning' offers a profound mediation on family, Jewish identity, and the meaning of home in a world constantly shaken by economic, social, and cultural change."― Henry Louis Gates Jr.



Doors open at 6 p.m. The discussion will be followed by an audience Q&A and book sales and signing with Watchung Booksellers. 

Open Book / Open Mind is sponsored by Montclair Public Library Foundation, Watchung Booksellers, Anonymous, Dr. Alex and Doris Malaspina, David and Mary Lee Jones, and our individual Underwriters. We are also grateful for the generous support of our in-kind sponsors, First Congregational Church of Montclair, The George, and Amanti Vino. To support Open Book / Open Mind and other library programs, click here to donate.

title: A Conversation with Analilia Mejia
description:
Temple Ner Tamid is pleased to host two special evenings with candidates for New Jersey’s 11th Congressional District. Join us for thoughtful conversations with Rabbi Marc Katz about the candidates’ vision for the future of America and New Jersey, with special attention to issues the Jewish community cares deeply about, including antisemitism, Israel, and synagogue security. 
On Monday, April 6 at 7:30pm, Rabbi Katz will be in conversation with Joe Hathaway. On Tuesday, April 7 at 7:30 pm, he will speak with Analilia Mejia. 
These evenings offer a meaningful opportunity to hear directly from the candidates in a community setting and to learn more about their priorities and perspectives. Guests are welcome to register for either night or attend both programs. 
The event is free and open to the public. 
This program will be available for streaming on the TNT YouTube channel. There is no need to register if you plan to live stream. 
The Temple Talks Series at Temple Ner Tamid is hosted by Rabbi Marc Katz and moderated by a nationally known journalist. The objective of the series is to engage our audience with discussions by current leaders on current issues that impact all communities. 
Click Here to Register!

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Expedition to the Black Sea and Ukraine NEW - Online
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register on the Adult School website.

What makes the Ukraine so important? The Black Sea area including Ukraine has been at the center of world events for thousands of years. Throughout history, there have been waves of invaders whose objective was to control this vital area. We will explore what makes this area so unique and vital by investigating the Ice Age, prehistory, invasions, migrations, language, landforms, coastline, and modern developments as well.

title: Tales on the Trails - Beauty of Trees
description:
Tales on the Trails 
Wednesdays, 1:00 PM -1:45 PM 
Age: Preschool 3-5 year olds 
April 8th – Beauty of Trees 
April’s Tales on the Trails class is all about trees. Learn about trees, their leaves, and the important role they play in our gardens. 
Discover the wonders of the outdoor world with your little one.  This program begins in the gardens of Van Vleck with a story, then continues with a walk and concludes with a simple craft or activity.  All programs focus on the theme listed.  A parent or caregiver must accompany each child.  Programs will be held outdoors, weather permitting. 
Sponsored by The Albert Payson Terhune Foundation  
Fee: $10/class per child for Members / $15/class per child for General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
  
Upcoming Classes 
May 27th – Sounds of Spring 
Do you hear the birds singing, the wind blowing, and rabbits scurrying around? These are all the sounds of spring. Join us for Tales on the Trails as we use our senses to explore the wonders of spring. 
  
June 17th – Magic of Moths 
What’s flying around the gardens? Is it a butterfly or could it be a moth? Learn about moths at this month’s Tales on the Trails.

title: CSAM Science Teaching Program - Program Meeting
description:
The CSAM Science Teaching Program holds biweekly meetings that are open, welcoming, and inclusive! Any student curious about science teaching is invited to join us. Each meeting highlights a theme related to science education, from classroom strategies to career pathways, and offers students opportunities to connect with mentors, explore internships, and gain real-world experience working with teachers and children. Students are encouraged to bring a guest who may also be interested in exploring science teaching.
These gatherings help us build a strong community of support, where students encourage one another, share experiences, and access valuable opportunities such as scholarships, paid internships, and mentoring from advanced peers and in-service science teachers. Whether you are just beginning to explore the idea of teaching or already on the path toward certification, these meetings are a place to grow, learn, and belong.

Hosted by: College of Science and Mathematics (CSAM)

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095722

title: IDEALS, INTERESTS, AND INTERNATIONAL ENGAGEMENT: THE UNITED STATES IN WORLD AFFAIRS
location: Presentation Hall (College of Communication and Media)
description:
America’s role in shaping the global political and economic order has shifted repeatedly over the last 250 years, reflecting the evolution of American interests and longstanding debates regarding the role of the United States in world affairs. Drawing on foundational principles, pivotal historical moments, and contemporary challenges, this lecture explores how American power, values, and institutions have influenced international norms, security, and governance and also how international dynamics have shaped American democracy. The lecture considers tensions between ideals and practices – democracy, markets, sovereignty, nation-building, imperialism, isolationism, intervention – in U.S. engagement abroad. Participants will be invited to reflect on the ways in which American leadership on the global stage has shaped our nation and what that implies in the current era.

Hosted by: Campus Activities, Civic & Voter Engagement, Campus Activities at Bloomfield

This month's presentation will be: Reducing Your Risk From Dementia presented by Jenna Noecker from Alzheimer NJ Currently, there is no surefire way to prevent dementia, but there is growing research that shows we can potentially reduce our risk for dementia by addressing lifestyle factors. This program explores what brain health is and describes practical, evidence-based lifestyle strategies - such as exercise, sleep, and social engagement - that can help support brain health over time. title: Board of Trustees Tuition Hearing
description:
Hey Red Hawks your voice matters, and we want to hear from you!
 
Each year, the Montclair State University Board of Trustees (BOT) hosts its Annual Tuition Hearing—a dedicated space for university community members to share their thoughts, ask questions, and express any concerns about potential increases in tuition, housing, and student fees for the upcoming academic year.
 
This open forum is not just about listening—it’s about understanding. During the hearing, University leaders will provide insight into how tuition and fee decisions are made, including the factors that influence these important choices. You’ll gain a clearer picture of the budgeting process and have the chance to directly engage with decision-makers.
 
Whether you have a personal story to share, a question you’ve been wanting to ask, or simply want to learn more, we encourage you to attend and participate. Your perspective helps shape a better, more inclusive student experience. All are welcome
 
💬 Come join the conversation. Be informed. Be heard. Be an Advocate
If you are unable to attend the BOT meeting on Thursday, April 9th or the listening sessions, submit your comments and/or questions on tuition and fees to: Quentin Ocampo at ocampoq1@montclair.edu or Jameson Klinsky at klinskyj1@montclair.edu .

Hosted by: Bloomfield College Student Government Association (BCSGA), Campus Activities, Civic & Voter Engagement, Student Government Association, Inc.

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12388648

This meditation technique can be quite helpful with reduction of stress and emotion regulation. We will cover the following topics: mindful breathing/body,

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405390

title: From Cosmos DB to AI: Search, Analytics & Intelligent Experiences
description:
Cloud Data Driven
**Turn transactional data into powerful insights and intelligent experiences 🚀**

Discover how to transform transactional data from Azure Cosmos DB into actionable insights and AI-driven applications. This session explores how to integrate Cosmos DB with Azure Synapse for advanced analytics and with Azure AI Search to enable discoverability, context-aware interactions, and a user-friendly interface for searching and retrieving data directly from Search.

Learn how to implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns and Agentic AI to build scalable, cloud-native solutions that maintain transactional integrity while ensuring high searchability and enterprise-grade security.

🔹 **Level:** Intermediate

▬▬▬▬▬ About the community ▬▬▬▬▬
📌Data Driven Community & Cloud Data-Driven user group
The Cloud Data-Driven User Group is the official user group of the Data-Driven Community, hosting weekly meetings as part of our community-driven activities. For a full list of upcoming events and more details about our community, visit: https://datadrivencommunity.com
▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬▬

title: Understanding Rights and Responsibilities— Immigration Policy
description:
As immigration policies develop, it is best to stay educated. Know your rights and responsibilities!
Join the New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children for a webinar on key legal and policy issues impacting the Montclair community. Information will be provided on immigration statuses, policy updates, legal protections, judicial vs. administrative orders, and understanding your rights if encountering law enforcement. Sessions are open for students, family, faculty, and staff in both English and Spanish. 
The New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children (NJCIC) is a state-wide legal and policy advocacy and education organization specializing in youth immigration. They provide objective legal resources, aid, and advice on immigration rights.
Student & Family English Session: March 25, 2:30-3:45: https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_FxYlh_kDRkCYCoOhfmEKyA#/registration
Student & Family Spanish Session: April 9, 5:30-6:45: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/82724283081?pwd=KzAjQw24vxqmzbURBXGmbBLV3V60m3.1

Hosted by: College for Education and Engaged Learning, Government Relations and Community Affairs , Office of Student Belonging, Office for Hispanic Initiatives, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections

title: NJ Real Estate Broker 30-Hour Office Management Course | Burgos Realty Academy
description: Registration Open NJ Real Estate Broker 30-Hour Office Management Course April 9 – April 23 | 5:30 PM – 9:30 PM Burgos Realty Academy offers the 30-hour Office Management Broker course as part of New Jersey’s required 150-hour Broker pre-licensing curriculum. This instructor-led course focuses on brokerage operations, supervision, compliance, risk management, and leadership responsibilities required to operate a real estate brokerage. The full 150-hour New Jersey Broker pre-licensing requirement includes: 30 Hours – Ethics & Agency 30 Hours – Office Management 90 Hours – Basic Broker Courses are taught by a licensed broker-owner, real estate instructor, and ARELLO Certified Distance Education Instructor. Instruction is designed to prepare students not only to pass the broker exam, but to manage, lead, and operate a compliant and effective brokerage.

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Jazz Library listening session: Sam Rivers
description: Join us for a dive into the music and life of a true creative force in modern jazz, Sam Rivers. Brilliant multi-instrumentalist, composer, heart of the New York jazz loft scene in the 1970s. Seed Artists founder Pheeroan akLaff, a colleague of Rivers, will guide us through the music, share stories, and pull excerpts from Rick Lopez's remarkable 768-page masterwork, Sam Rivers Sessionography: A Work in Progress. We listen. We talk. We listen some more. This event will be held on the first floor, Plymouth Street side.

title: Raised Bed Gardening with Bracco Farms
description:
Ready to take your gardening game to the next level? Join us for an engaging and informative talk all about the power of the Raised Bed Garden!

This talk will be hosted by Bracco Farms. They will discuss the benefits of raised garden beds include better soil drainage, easy access to plants, portability, soil customization increased soil control, and also the ability to keep weeds and pests at bay. Bracco Farms will discuss everything from planning to growing in a Raised Bed Garden. We will demonstrate how almost every kind of plant will grow better in it for many reasons which we will point out. They will also bring, show and demonstrate many of the hand tools used in a Raised Bed garden.

Please note this event will take place in the Main Library's Auditorium.

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

description:
Viola Masterclass with Melissa Reardon
Free and Open to the Public
Grammy-nominated violist Melissa Reardon of the Borromeo String Quartet will lead a masterclass in viola performance and chamber music collaboration, and expressive string interpretation.
Bio
Grammy-nominated violist Melissa Reardon is an internationally renowned performer whose solo and chamber playing spans all musical genres. Melissa is the Artistic Director of the Portland Chamber Music Festival in Portland, ME, violist of the Borromeo String Quartet, Artist-in-Residence at Bard College and Conservatory, and a founding member and Executive Director of the East Coast Chamber Orchestra (ECCO).

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

title: 🥾 Let's Hike!! the Turtle Back Trail loop
description:
Let's Walk!!
Come walk 2 or 4 miles along woods trails in South Mountain Reservation near the Turtle Back Rock. We'll take parts of the Hillspur and Turtle Back trails to make 2 custom loops. Stay for one or both 😁.

These trails are gentle, **but are not for beginners**. If you've walked with us at Eagle Rock, this is similar in difficulty. There may be wet or muddy sections and lots of rocks and tree roots. Please wear hiking boots/shoes and bring trekking poles or a walking stick.

Don't forget your water!

Inclement weather may cancel this event. Please check the event page for comments prior to leaving for the meetup.

**Please arrive 5 minutes early so we can start on time.**

**Disclaimer:** If you join this event, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap.

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405391

Join us for an enchanting Cream Tea in the Living Room at Van Vleck House! 
Upon arrival, you will be offered a menu of fine teas and infusions. Your choice of tea will be brewed to perfection. Once everyone is settled in, the tiers will arrive with freshly baked scones, jam, homemade clotted cream, scrumptious sandwiches, and a dessert. We hope you will join us! 
This event is for adults and children ages 13+. For group bookings of more than 8 guests, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. Seatings are 90 minutes, and registration is required. 
Dietary Restrictions
A gluten-free option is available with prior notice. We are not able to accommodate a dairy-free or vegan diet. All requests must be communicated to us 48 hours before the event. You may note this request on the checkout page. For dietary/allergy inquiries, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. 
Cancellation Policy
We are unable to accommodate cancellations or rescheduling. If you find you are no longer able to attend your scheduled event, you may transfer your ticket(s) to another person(s) to attend instead. Please notify Van Vleck House and Gardens so we can update your ticket name(s) for event check-in. 
Please note that this event is a fundraiser, and a portion of your ticket price will go towards supporting our beloved Van Vleck House & Gardens. 
Member Price: $45
General Price: $55
A 10% gratuity for your server is included in tea pricing.

title: Summer Lifeguard Hiring
description:
Campus Recreation is hiring for the summer!
We want qualified staff to work during the summer at our 3 facilities on campus. 
Not certified? We can train you! 
If you work full time, we provide housing for free at the Village apartments!
Apply today
Applications due by 4/10!
All employees must be current students, registered for Fall 2026. Incoming students can be hired starting July 1st. We offer part-time and full-time hours at both indoor and outdoor facilities. We will work around class schedules, but all staff must be able to work a minimum of 14 hours (part-time), and 32 hours (full-time). Full time staff get free housing at the Village.
Currently certified lifeguards - we accept all major certifications, and can recertify you for free. 
Non-certified students - Students that are not currently certified can take a pretest for free, before you register for a lifeguard training course. If you pass the pretest, and can work a minimum of 14 hours per week for the summer (average), than we will certify you for free. If you can work full-time, we may also be able to offer free housing for the summer (space is limited, so this option is first-come, first-serve).
Swim tests will take place between March 23rd and April 10th, 2026. 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

title: Spring Retreat
description:
Join Campus Activities for our Spring Student Retreat  a chance to step away from campus, connect with other students, and enjoy a day filled with activities, good food, and a refreshing change of scenery. This trip is designed to help you relax, recharge, and make new memories while participating in group experiences and social opportunities throughout the day.
The trip is open to current Bloomfield/Montclair State students only. Spots are limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis.
A $5 (cash only) reservation fee is required to secure your spot at the time of sign-up.
Transportation will be provided, and the bus will depart from and return to the Bloomfield Campus. All meals are included, and some snacks will be provided. Students are encouraged to wear comfortable clothing and shoes suitable for a full day of activities.
Additional trip details, including itinerary information and preparation instructions, will be shared with students once their registration and reservation are confirmed.
 
To sign up you must go to the Bloomfield Campus - Campus Activities located in Talbott Hall

title: Erev Shabbat Service
description:
Erev Shabbat services begin on Friday at 6:30pm. These services are especially musical, often with a band of congregants accompanying the Cantor. Usually around an hour, these services are filled with a lot of spirit. It’s not uncommon to see people dancing in their seat or hear the whole congregation clapping their hands. Friday night is intergenerational. Glance around and see senior citizens sitting beside religious school families both getting something out of the services.  
In the summer months, services are led by members of the congregation with support from our clergy.
Join us most weeks from anywhere for Erev Shabbat Services through Zoom or our YouTube Livestream.

title: Friday Night Baseball Watch Party - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
Come join the group to celebrate the third weekend of baseball at The Shepherd & The Knucklehead of Hoboken, 1313 Willow Ave.

New York Mets and Yankees games start at 7:10 p.m.

Of course, join us at any time. All you gotta do is show up and have a good time hanging with familiar faces and meeting new ones. It's totally optional to wear your favorite baseball gear or consume a few adult beverages.

Please be smart about travel. The train station is not far away and runs late. Parking can be a challenge, but there is street parking (where residential permit is not required) and several paid public parking garages, including at 112 River St. and 1330 Willow Ave. Feel free to comment below if interested in carpooling or have questions about travel options.

Questions? Suggestions? Concerns? You can reach out to Andy Milone at 201-234-9609 (call/text), or by message over Meetup or Whatsapp.

Hope you'll consider attending one of our bowling, volleyball, table tennis, skating or other activities -> [www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball](https://www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball)

title: Free Trip to Historic Philadelphia
description:
Join the History Club for a full-day trip to Philadelphia as we explore some of the most historic places in the United States.
We’ll visit Independence Hall and the Liberty Bell, followed by stops at the Museum of the American Revolution and the National Constitution Center. Together, these sites tell the story of the nation’s founding. We'll learn about the debates and ideals that shaped the country and how those ideas have been interpreted, challenged, and expanded over time.
This trip is an opportunity to reflect on the past 250 years of American history while also thinking about the kind of country we hope to build in the next 250.
To wrap up the day, we’ll head to Dave and Buster’s to relax, grab dinner, and spend time together before returning home.
This trip is part of Montclair State University’s Montclair 250 celebrations.

Hosted by: The Historical Society at Montclair State

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12377877

title: The Elementary Spacetime Show
location: Alexander Kasser Theater, Montclair State University, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
A highly inventive new musical by César Alvarez and Emily Orling, The Elementary Spacetime Show tells the story of Alameda: a young girl who enters a liminal void somewhere in between life and death. Alameda must pass through the eight existential trials of a cosmic Vaudevillian game show to discover the possibility that her darkness is a gift. Her journey is set to a kaleidoscopic contemporary score that bravely handles the subject of teenage suicide with both sensitivity and vibrant showmanship, blending sly humor with emotional nuance.

title: Tri Sigma Initiation
description:
Tri Sigma initiation is a meaningful ceremony that marks the transition from new member to fully initiated sister within the sorority. It is a formal and reflective experience centered on the organization’s values of wisdom, power, faith, hope, and love. During initiation, members learn more deeply about Tri Sigma’s traditions, history, and lifelong commitment to sisterhood, service, and personal growth. The ceremony symbolizes belonging, responsibility, and the shared bond that connects all Tri Sigma members across chapters and generations.

title: Let's Walk!! Brookdale Park
description:
Let's Walk!!
If entering from Bellevue Avenue and park in the first parking lot on the left. We will be gathering near the the playground area.

if entering from Watchung Ave, park and walk up to the upper parking lot near the playground area.

We will be walking the outer path. There are several break-off points if you do not want to walk the entire path.

The walk will be about an hour or at least 3-4 miles.

Wear comfortable shoes!

Please plan to arrive 5 minutes early. The walk will start promptly.

Brookdale Park offers the public access to both passive and active recreational activities as well as picturesque vistas. Trails for walking and running, lush groves and extensive lawns, playgrounds, sports fields, track facilities, tennis courts, an archery range and stadium grandstand are just some of the numerous attractions that make Brookdale Park a magnet for nature lovers, families, and physical fitness enthusiasts for miles around.

https://www.essexcountyparks.org/parks/brookdale-park

title: Outdoor Adventures - Arbor Day Everyday
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Saturday, 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
April 11th – Arbor Day Everyday 
Arbor Day is a day to reflect on the importance of trees in our environment. We have an abundance of beautiful trees throughout the gardens. At April’s Outdoor Adventures, learn more about our trees as we explore their role in our environment, their unique leaf shapes, and what animals use trees as their homes.  
Join us in the gardens as you and your family explore and learn about the outdoor world through crafts, scavenger hunts, activity sheets and educational displays. Each program will focus on a specific theme, and our Van Vleck Education team will guide your family through this interactive learning experience. This program is entirely outdoors, weather dependent. Registration is required and includes everything you need to participate in the program. Designed for children ages 4 -11 but all are welcome. 
Fee: FREE for Members of Van Vleck; $15 per family  for  General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
Sponsored by  
  
Upcoming Outdoor Adventures: 
May 16th – Honeybees & Hummingbirds 
What do bees and hummingbirds have in common? They fly and they are pollinators! Join us to learn about your local pollinators, how they make a positive impact on our environment, and what we can do to help them on their mission.  
  
June 6th – Insect Safari  
You can find many different insects buzzing, wiggling, and crawling around the gardens. Join us for an Insect Safari at this month’s Outdoor Adventures. Create a bug inspired craft, take a hike looking for insects, and learn about the important roles insects play in the garden.

title: Daytime Pool at Clifton Billiards
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
***Trying something different this time, Saturday afternoon date and in the window for day passes! So if you are available and feel like staying longer, or coming closer to the middle or end, it's more flexible, easier to change between tables and play with more people and less time is spent counting hours, etc. We'll see how this pans out going forward.***

***Meeting Point:***
Outside of the building; After 5-10 minutes: You can meet us upstairs at the pool tables. *\*\*Please look for the group as the staff do not keep up with the group\*\***
*** Price:
Photo of the day passes pricing is posted, however if you only want to stay a short while and would rather pay less, the price is $6.50 per hour per person if you have 4 players at your table. However, the final price is based on the total amount of minutes played. At the end of the hour, the table amount will be split per person. Each table paying this way should have a table leader, **this person will collect the money of players at their table and will pay for the table on one card.** 3-4 table leaders will be needed, **volunteering for this is encouraged**

***Bringing cash is highly encouraged to pay your portion to your table leader***

*Parking*: street parking is available however it may be hard to find close to the venue. There are side streets before the town proper, but a good option is the nearby ANT bookstore which has a lot which should be okay to use.

***This location has food & drinks available for purchase.***

*** Expectations**:
Pool cues are available at the location, expect mid to low lighting. If you come after 7:15pm, look for the Meetup group upstairs.
** *What if I'm a beginner or only want to play with people at a similar level?**
Tables will be divided between beginners, intermediate players, and advanced. However, this depends on how many types of players come, so it will be divided as best as possibly available. If you would like game rules, please speak with Marc.** Marc is also available to answer questions including how to hold the cue, stances, etc.**
*** Is anyone at Clifton Billiards after the event is over?
Afterward, most go home or go to a local bar. If you'd like to hang out after, definitely speak with members to see if they want to do something after.
*** Can I switch tables?**
*Yes, but it does make it easier if you stay at your table. If you switch tables, pay the amount of time you were at that table. Example, if you were at able A for an hour, pay the hour to the table A leader. If you go table B for two hours, pay your two hours to the table B leader. At the end, the table leader is taking the payments for the total amount of time at that table.*
*** How do I get balls for the table and how many people do I tell staff that I have?*
*One person per table gives their ID to the staff in order to check out balls. Again, the price for 4 people is about $6.50. If you have more or less, it slightly changes. So it just depends on how many people are at your table. It may be better to mention that you have more people esp if you are starting with 4 or 5 people.*
*** I feel like people aren't trying to be my friend.*
*Please remember that different people have different goals. Some may come to just play billiards, others may be there to meet new people or do something social, and some may be there to make long term friends. Find people that match your goals or be ok with others that may not.
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Lego Robotics for Elementary Students
location: RecRoom, 1 Lackawanna Plaza, Ste. 6, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
LEGO Robotics for Elementary Kids (Grades K–5) 
LEGO Robotics at Kinetic Minds introduces kids to engineering and beginner coding through hands-on building, testing, and problem-solving using LEGO robotics kits. 
In this small-group, drop-in friendly workshop, kids work in pairs to build real robots, program simple behaviors, and improve their designs through guided challenges. The focus is on curiosity, experimentation, and learning how things work—there’s no single “right” build and no step-by-step cookie-cutter instructions. 
Workshops are intentionally capped at a low student-to-instructor ratio to support collaboration, confidence, and meaningful learning. 
Kids will explore:
• Engineering and building with LEGO robotics components
• Beginner coding concepts (motors, sensors, cause and effect)
• Testing, debugging, and iterating designs
• Teamwork, communication, and explaining ideas 
Details:
• 60-minute session
• Small-group format
• No prior experience required 
Community Sponsorship:
20% of seats are reserved for community sponsorship. Families interested in sponsored spots can email info@kineticmindscreative.com
for details.

title: NJ Code & Coffee - April meetup
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
Calling all New Jersey techies to the **NJ Code and Coffee** tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious).
That includes operators, founders, and investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... **all are welcome!**

People of all skill and experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

Come grab a cup of coffee and meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, **make friends!**

**Agenda:**
**2pm:** Doors open, networking starts
**2:30pm:** Intro circle
**3pm:** networking continues/ (optional) "**Build Your Own ai Agent**" workshop ([more details here](https://www.meetup.com/nj-code-coffee/events/314048120))
**5pm:** event ends

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle:**
We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, and logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says:

* your name and job title/ role in tech/ tech stack,
* What you can help others with,
* What you would like help with,
* Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool.

After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized.

**Community Discord - come hang out before and after!:**
[www.codeandcoffee.chat](www.codeandcoffee.chat)

**The Venue**
Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming and a powerful network.

Headquartered in the City of Newark, their share-space provides resources for startups, coaching for tech companies, and a wealth of intellectuals to collaborate and build with. The next wave in enterprises is being born within their walls. Become a part of the story: [EqualSpace.co/](https://equalspace.co/)

title: Building Your Own AI Agent: A Guided Walkthrough
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
As part of [April's monthly meetup](Building Your Own AI Agent: A Guided Walkthrough), NJ Code & Coffee presents "**Building Your Own AI Agent: A Guided Walkthrough**".

Join us for a guided walk through of the core principles of AI agents: completions, tools, and the agentic loop.

Whether you're AI-curious or already shipping with LLMs, you'll leave with a solid mental model of how agents actually work under the hood.

We'll use the Anthropic Claude API with the TypeScript SDK as our foundation, building up from a simple completion all the way to a fully autonomous agent.

Bring your laptop if you want to follow along; there'll be one hands-on moment where we build the agent loop together.

**All skill levels are welcome.**

**What You'll Learn:**

* How LLM completions actually work under the hood
* How to give agents tools and let them decide when to use them
* The agentic loop — how agents reason, act, and iterate autonomously
* How to build a working agent from scratch using the Anthropic TypeScript SDK
* Mental models for thinking about agents that you can apply to any framework

**What You’ll Need To Follow Along:**

* A laptop with the latest version of Node.js and git installed
* An Anthropic API key with a few dollars of credits loaded

**Doors open at 2pm, talk starts at 3, arrive early for networking and light refreshments.**
Bring your laptop.

**Speaker Bio:**

Aza Walker is a self-taught software engineer with a fascination for computers and the intricate systems that make them work.
He enjoys designing and building large-scale applications, and currently works at ShipBob as a Senior Software Engineer, where he focuses on developing scalable microservices.

and 4pm Film Screening at The Clairidge Theatre –
YOU GOT GOLD: A CELEBRATION OF JOHN PRINE 
You Got Gold: A Celebration of John Prine
captures a star-studded tribute to the legendary songwriter, filmed in October 2022 at Nashville’s Ryman Auditorium. The event brought together acclaimed artists, friends, and family to share behind the scenes stories and perform classic songs, honoring Prine’s enduring legacy. Prine, widely celebrated as one of history’s greatest songwriters, captivated millions of fans and earned the admiration of icons such as Bonnie Raitt, Brandi Carlile, Tyler Childers, Lucinda Williams, Dwight Yoakam, Jason Isbell, and Bob Weir, amongst many others, are featured in the film performing on the Ryman stage for this special celebration of his life and music.

title: Chester County Pastoral Hike
description:
Metrotrails: NJ, PA, NY Metropolitan Area Hiking and More!
Join us for this excellent rural hike in Chester County PA, which ties together a different way the recent Chester County hikes we've been doing.
From the start, we will do the easier back roads.
The only lined road we will be on is the very beginning.
We will walk through the historic Sheeder-Hall Covered Bridge, then out to the main road for under a half mile, We then turn onto some back roads including Sheeder Mill Road, HIlltop Road, Birchrun Road, Green Lane, and Bartlett Road.
At least half of this is unpaved, and none of these named roads even have lines in them, making them narrow, lightly used, and very pleasant.
We'll soon get on the Horse-Shoe Trail from where we left off. This takes us through pleasant woods, and then out into cultivated farm fields.
Bits of the Horse-Shoe Trail also follow these little used roads.
We'll continue on the HST to Art School Lane, then make our way east and pass the historic Vincent Baptist Church and cemetery.
We'll continue to the intersection with Miller Road, where we'll begin following the Bryn Coed Trail System.
This amazing and long trail system weaves though woods and fields keeping with the overall theme of the hike.
Amazing pastoral views will make for a relaxing walk that will lead us almost all the way back to our stating point.
We'll get on one more dirt road before the end, and then an abandoned road that is now in the trail system right back to where we parked.
Distance 15 mi
If needed, contact me at 908 343 8374

title: SMC Trail Work Volunteer Day- CHANGED DATE
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**TRAIL WORK** will begin this year with finishing work on the Lenape Trail reroute below the Beech Brook Cascades. The steps are in (see photo), but final tread improvements on switchbacks and throughout are needed. We will meet at the Dog Park (11 Bear Lane) and head down to the Lenape trail from there.

Trail work is physically challenging but very satisfying as we use hand tools to build and repair trails. Participants must be 15+ years. Those younger than 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Participants should wear long pants due to [ticks](https://www.somocon.org/?page_id=193928&fb) and sturdy shoes, and bring gloves (though backup gloves will be available). Tools, water, and a snack will be provided. (Registration is not required though if you sign up on Meetup, it helps us know how many tools to bring.)

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405392

title: Croton Aqueduct Bike Trip
description:
Get ready for a scenic spin through history as we ride the legendary Old Croton Aqueduct! This unforgettable bike trip takes you along one of New York’s most beautiful and peaceful trails, where tree-lined paths, and charming river views, create the perfect backdrop for a day outdoors. As we cruise along the trail you’ll enjoy plenty of photo-worthy stops along the way. Expect a relaxed pace with time to explore, snack, and soak in the scenery. Whether you're in it for the fresh air, the history, or just a joyful ride with great company, this Croton Aqueduct adventure is a perfect blend of nature, culture, and fun on two wheels! 
How do I sign-up?

Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students - $10 | Faculty/Staff - $20

When and Where?

Sunday, April 12, 2026
10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Croton Aqueduct, NJ - Begins and ends at Student Recreation Center

What to bring:

Money for spending, lunch, water, comfortable shoes, sunscreen.

Potential Hazards: Hazardous weather, poisonous plants/allergies, insect bites, unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, and injuries, including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations and fractures.
 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12267448

title: Rug Tufting Workshop
location: The Eclectic Chic Boutique, 547 Bloomfield Avenue, Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Learn how to make your own custom rug! This rug tufting class will guide you through creating your own custom rug using a tufting gun. You’ll learn essential techniques for handling the tufting gun safely and effectively, focusing entirely on mastering the craft since your design and yarn colors will already be prepared on the tufting frame. Once tufting is complete, you’ll discover how to apply glue and backing to secure and protect your rug. The class finishes with the exciting step of trimming excess yarn, revealing the final texture and look of your handmade rug. This hands-on experience combines creativity with practical skills to help you craft a unique, personalized piece. 
Upon booking you will receive a link to choose your rug design for the class. 
This is a great opportunity to make a fun gift, to keep or to give. Invite your partner, friends, family, or coworkers for a fun and creative day out. 
Bring a mask if you are sensitive to yarn particles and chemical smells from glue.

Second Sunday Concert: Teatro Nuovo
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
April 12, 2PM 
Led by conductor/musicologist Will Crutchfield, Teatro Nuovo – Italian for “new theater” – is a young organization devoted to cutting-edge interpretation of classic Italian opera.  Since its debut in 2018, Teatro Nuovo has presented thirteen operas by Rossini, Donizetti, Bellini, Verdi, Ricci, and Mayr, culminating in the modern premiere of Carolina Uccelli’s Anna di Resburgo, which made the New York Times list of the 20 best Classical events for 2024.  This year Teatro Nuovo will celebrate the bicentennial of Italian opera in America.

This concert will show how delving into forgotten traditions can animate the future of the art, as Will Crutchfield and young vocal stars will perform virtuoso thrillers and heart-melting lyrics from the golden age of Italian Bel Canto. Don’t miss this exciting opportunity to hear some of opera’s newest rising stars! 
Members received the registration link in mid-March. This concert is free but a donation of $10 is appreciated to continue to fund the series. Registrations are recommended but there are always a few seats available just before the start of the concert. 

 
Many thanks to our Series Patrons.

title: Learn How to Get Published with the Book Doctors
description:
As part of its ongoing writers’ workshop series, Watchung Booksellers hosts authors Arielle Eckstut and David Henry Sterry, aka The Book Doctors, to share insights on how to get published. The workshop will be held on Sunday, April 12 from 6-8:30 p.m. 
This workshop will take you through the four primary paths to publication, choosing the right idea, crafting an attention-getting pitch and query, and finding the right agent/publisher. You’ll be armed with knowledge before pitching your idea at Pitch-a-palooza during the Montclair Lit Fest! 
David Henry Sterry & Arielle Eckstut are co-founders of the Book Doctors. They are co-authors of The Essential Guide to Getting Your Book Published: How To Write It, Sell It, and Market It… Successfully. Tickets are $80 for the class and can be purchased at watchungbooksellers.com/events/writers-workshops

title: Gotta Sing: BROADWAY!
location: Just Jake’s, 30 Park St., Montclair, NJ, 07042, United States
description:
Fun, themed Sing Along Party for GenXers, Baby Boomers and Beyond! Think of Gotta Sing as the opposite of karaoke: no one is handed a mic to sing all alone—we display the lyrics to the entire room and everyone sings together. And, unlike most karaoke, we don’t use canned, knockoff accompaniment—we play the best-known recordings, and our guests sing along with the original artists! For each event, we curate 90 minutes’ worth of fan favorites; and with the lyrics projected on a large screen, everyone will want to sing along!

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405394

title: Mah Jongg Meetup: Spring Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following meetups.

The Mah Jongg Meetup provides a weekly opportunity for players to continue practicing their established skills, sharpen their strategies, build confidence at the game table, and socialize with fellow Mah Jongg players!

The library will provide five reserved tables in the cafe! 

NOTE: Attendees should bring their personal sets to this meetup and should already have an understanding of the Mah Jongg game. Attendees should bring their 2025 or 2026 National Mah Jongg League card to the meetup. Bringing an additional set is encouraged!

*Note: There will be a limited number of Mah Jongg sets available on a first-come, first-served basis.

If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here.

 

IMPORTANT REMINDERS:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 
	Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space - Weekly Monday Morning - NEW
description:
The Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space provides a weekly opportunity for teaching yourself the basics of American Mah Jongg at your own pace.

The library will provide the book Beginner's Guide to American Mah Jongg by Elaine Sandberg, Mah Jongg card, a set and table!  You can also boost your learning with a list of  Mah Jongg resources available at the library. 

After you know the basics of Mah Jongg, and would like to play the game with other Mah Jongg players, please register for the "Mah Jongg Meetup" by clicking here.

title: Memory Café - A Dementia-Friendly Social Gathering
description:
For adults with dementia and their caregivers, this event offers a welcoming, safe, calming space for socializing, music, art and reminiscing every month.


A “Memory Café” is a dementia-friendly social gathering for individuals with early-stage memory loss or mild cognitive decline and their care givers.

The meetings will be held in a safe and comfortable environment where people can interact and socialize with others having similar experiences. At the gatherings participants can get support in a non-judgmental setting, while enjoying the benefits of music for the brain.

The meetings will be run by Lisy Espindola, LCSW and Kim McLennon, MSW and music therapist. Lunch and/or snacks will be served.

This is a joint program between Lifelong Montclair at the Township of Montclair and the Montclair Public Library.

Attendance is free but registration is required. Questions? Call 973-744-3278.

To stay informed, sign up for one or all of our eNewsletters, click here.

Photographs may be taken during our programs and events for promotional purposes such as social media and e-newsletters. If you do not wish to have your photograph taken please let us know before the event.

title: Free Swim Lessons *Beginner*
description:
Brief Description: 
Never swam before? No problem! These lessons will give you the important foundational skills in swimming and help you be more comfortable in the pool. Learn how to use proper breath control, float, and swim with a kickboard. 
Skills Focus:

Being comfortable in the water
Learning how to float on your back and front
Learning how to kick off the wall with a kickboard
Introducing proper breath control in the water
Learning how to tread water
Learn alternate kicks (Froggy Kick)
Being comfortable in the deep end 
Introducing arms to your strokes

How do I Sign-up?

Registration is not required
FREE with a valid Montclair State ID

When and Where?

Student Recreation Center - Pool

Swimming unassisted on your front and back 
Improve treading (in the deep end)
Improving breath control in the water
Learn alternate kicks (Dolphin Kick)
Introduction to and/or improving your breaststroke

Introduction to open and flip turns

title: Community and Social Services Career Expo
description:
Community and Social Services Career ExpoDate: April 13, 2026Time: 3:30pm-6:30pmLocation: University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)
Registration is available on Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/37dfb726-9582-4e23-9c82-6561adbc3e11/student_preview
 
Description:
Calling all community and service-minded students exploring clinical and non-clinical career options!
 
Are you looking for a job or internship? Are you curious about the career paths you can pursue with your major or graduate program? If so, the Community and Social Services Career Expo is an amazing opportunity to meet employers that align with your professional interests. Whether you are a freshman or a graduate student, this event is designed to help you explore career possibilities, gain networking experience by talking with employers, and make connections that may eventually lead to a job or internship. There will be fun mixed in with the opportunity to win raffle prizes! 
 
More Information:- Mark your calendar! Monday, April 13, 3:30pm - 6:30pm, University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)- Visit with non-profit, government, and corporate employers that have a service-oriented mission- Discover opportunities with campus organizations and departments, including international experiences that incorporate social impact- Pre-registration is strongly encouraged - Register on Handshake!- Event details and employer list can be found on Handshake. - Dress neatly and bring resumes! Check out Rocky's Closet for free professional attire. 
 
This event is ideal for graduate and undergraduate students seeking jobs and internships in: Counseling, Child Advocacy, Justice Studies, Social Work, Political Science, Psychology and so much more!
 
Co-sponsored by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences, College for Community Health, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, Office of Experiential Education and Career Connections, Office of Government Relations, Office of International Academic Initiatives
 
Questions? Contact Jennifer Dudeck-Lenis at dudecklenisj@montclair.edu.
 

Hosted by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS), College for Community Health, Government Relations and Community Affairs , Office of International Academic Initiatives, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections

title: Practical Spanish 1
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is for those of you who know little to no Spanish and are interested in acquiring some practical communication skills. You will be introduced to basic greetings and practice useful questions and answers. You will learn the fundamentals of Spanish pronunciation while mastering introductory grammar and vocabulary.
 
Topics covered:


	conversation/questions
	greetings
	personal information
	expressions of courtesy
	feelings
	asking for direction
	talk about needs and wants
	where people are from
	Alphabet
	Numbers/days/date
	Colors
	Basic commands
	Some ar/er/ir  regular verbs
	Verb to be: ser/estar
	Verb to go: ir
	Verbs: tener/necesitar/querer
	Ir   a
	Negation
	Subject pronouns/difference between tú/usted
	Gender and number of nouns/definite/indefinite articles
	Some adjectives including cuánto/a
	The expression “Hay”
	Basic geography, culture and food

title: Italian Conversation - Online
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is appropriate for those who have some basic knowledge of Italian. Each class focuses on the use of Italian and cultural knowledge for communicating in a variety of situations including shopping, sightseeing, making reservations, dining out and having a simple conversation with others.  Students will speak using the present tense and will be introduced to simple grammar to express past time.  We will review many relevant expressions and vocabulary in context. This course will be appropriate for students who have completed Italian for Travelers 1 and 2 or Basic Italian 1 & 2.

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
*
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.
source: Meetup: Board Game Night at Verona Inn

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405395

title: Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair**  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Join baseball historian Sam Bernstein for a lively survey of Jewish involvement in America’s National Pastime.  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e303368f110026c0c410  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
  
**About the Program**  
Using audio and video, *Jews and Baseball* explores the origins of the game and the impact Jewish players, owners, and innovators have had on its evolution. The program highlights figures such as Hank Greenberg, Sandy Koufax, Barney Dreyfuss, Bud Selig, Lou Boudreau, Marvin Miller, as well as the game's current Jewish stars. It also delves into topics including antisemitism in the sport, baseball’s growth in Israel, and the lore of the “Koufax Curse.”  
  
**About Sam Bernstein**  
Sam Bernstein is a retired Social Worker who has published many articles on baseball history. He received a BA in History from the American University and an MSW from Adelphi University. After a career serving the Jewish community at JCCs and Jewish Family Service, Sam worked for the Elizabeth Public Schools and as an Instructor in Social Work for Rutgers University. He was raised in Malverne, NY and moved to New Jersey with his wife Maureen in 1999.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Lunch With the Rabbi
description:
Join us for our monthly “Lunch with the Rabbi” class where each year a group of dedicated congregants meet and discuss a provocative Jewish book. This year we will be reading Rabbi Katz’s own Yochanan’s Gamble: Judaism’s Pragmatic Approach to Life. The book makes the claim that rather than have the intractable, strident discourse of today’s politics, we need to embrace the pragmatism of the Rabbis, which began with Yochanan ben Zakkai’s decision to trade Jerusalem of Yavneh. In essence, we can benefit today from the model of the ancient rabbis who make messy compromises, lead (but not too far ahead of their flock), change laws if they don’t work, and preserve peace within their community in order to better function in our day of division. 
Going forward, the class will meet monthly on the 2nd Tuesday of each month. Brown bag lunch (dairy or pareve) at 12pm, hybrid learning at 12:30pm. 
REGISTER HERE!

title: Bible Study (Guild Room)
description:
From Daytime Bible Study Leader Paul N:
During Daytime Bible Study, we discuss the Bible and books that analyze the Bible in depth. We also have fun and food. In 2024, we studied Mark and screened films Sabbath and 1946: The Mistranslation That Shifted A Culture. We read books about Holy Week, the Sabbath, The Sermon On The Mount, and Biblical women. All reference resources, translations, and interpretations are welcome. 
Current Book: “Who Stole My Bible? Reclaiming Scripture As A Handbook For Resisting Tyranny” by Rev. Jennifer Butler. 
Past Bible Study Books Have Included:
Sabbath As Resistance by Walter Brueggeman
Entering The Passion Of Jesus by Amy-Jill Levine
Jesus’ Alternative Plan: The Sermon On The Mount by Richard Rohr

title: Everything Book Club, "Happiness" by Aminatta Forna
location: Montclair Main - Tower Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
A meditation in fiction on love, trauma, chance, and the nature of wild things--perfect for Earth Day. A Ghanaian psychiatrist and an American biologist who studies urban foxes bump into each other on Waterloo Bridge in London and their fates entwine when they get involved in a search for a missing boy. By a Scottish and Sierra Leonean writer whose books have been translated into 22 languages.  

“An exquisite novel about how chance and love connect us.”—The Washington Post

Aminatta Forna is a Scottish and Sierra Leonean writer whose books have been translated into 22 languages. She spent periods of her childhood in Iran, Thailand and Zambia. She is also the author of three other novels, including "The Memory of Love" as well as two works of non-fiction. She is a Fellow of the Royal Society of Literature

title: (CANCELED) Practical Spanish 3: Basic Conversation
description:

This class is for those of you who are able to communicate in Spanish at a novice level, but want to continue to improve your pronunciation and your knowledge of the language while increasing your vocabulary. Presentations, conversations and dialogues will be used as techniques for oral practice. Grammatical structures will be introduced and reviewed as needed.
 
Topics covered:


	Music/songs/artists
	Cooking/Food description/Meals
	Family
	Daily routine with reflexive verbs/ accessories
	Past tense (preterite) of regular verbs and some irregular verbs
