title: Writing: Method & Motivation
location: Montclair Main - Teen Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

A workshop for anyone who wants to write, or wants to infuse their existing writing practice with new energy. In each class, we'll consider one element of fiction or creative non-fiction, such as point of view, narrative voice, and what makes a piece of writing riveting, or boring. Students can expect in-class writing prompts, reading aloud, and optional homework. Positive encouragement, rather than formal critiques, will be featured, and inspiration and organization for writers will be discussed. Students should bring whatever they use to write to each class.

title: 5K Training - No Boundaries - Orientation Night
location: Montclair - Anderson Park, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class.

Click here to register for the early bird price, available until April 3rd.

Click here for registrations April 3rd and later.

Have you always wanted to run or walk a 5K but didn't know how to begin training? Maybe you are nervous or don't want to do it alone? Maybe you have had a hard time sticking to a training program in the past? If you answered "Yes" to any of the above, our iconic beginner program is perfect for you. Often the first step is the hardest.

The camaraderie of a group and experience of your coaches and mentors provide great support toward healthier habits and lifestyle. This program is designed for people just starting a walking/running routine for the first time, restarting after a winter hiatus and those who want to run faster or longer. In 8 weeks, meeting twice a week, our fun, unintimidating program will help you walk, walk-run or run across the finish line at the Fitzgerald 5k Lager Run in June.

In collaboration with Dawn and John Fabbro of Fleet Feet Montclair

title: Awakening from Grief - Cancelled
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teaching and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous.

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12405397

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: 2026 Class Gift Tabling - Information and Philanthropy Cord Pick-Up
description:
Each year, our graduating class participates in a proud Class Gift tradition where members of the class invest in the University by supporting areas of Montclair State University that are most important to them. We invite the Class of 2026 to come together to show your support!
All gifts to Montclair count! Make your gift to any area of campus you feel most passionate about – such as the Emergency Book Fund, The Red Hawk Pantry, your school or college, The Fund for Montclair State or athletic program.
Your gift will pay it forward for future Red Hawks and kick-start your philanthropic connection to Montclair.

Commemorate your class year! Make a gift of at least $20.26 and you’ll receive your customized philanthropy cord to wear over your commencement robe. 
For a gift of $40, celebrate this milestone with your family, friends and colleagues by having your message displayed on the screen at the Commencement you will be participating in, and receive a customized philanthropy cord.

How do I get my customized philanthropy cord?
Customized philanthropy cords will be available for pick-up:

Tuesday, October 28 from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Thursday, November 20  from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Tuesday, December 2 from 12:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Bloomfield Campus Graduation Fair: Wednesday, December 10th from 12:00 – 6:00 p.m. in Deacon’s Den, Talbott Hall (for Bloomfield graduates only)
Montclair Campus Graduation Fairs: Thursday, December 11th from 10:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m. in the Student Center Ballrooms (for Montclair or Bloomfield graduates)

Graduates can also arrange to pick up their philanthropy cords in the Overlook Corporate Center, 150 Clove Road, Little Falls, NJ during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Philanthropy cords can also be mailed to those who cannot pick it up. The last day we will mail the cords is Monday, December 22, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving at stewartj@montclair.edu or 973-655-5328 to finalize options to get your cords for the Winter Commencement.
Every student has benefitted from donor generosity to areas of academic support, emergency funding for students and overall priorities to boost students’ success every day. Thank you for paying it forward!For more information, please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving, at 973-655-5328 or stewartj@montclair.edu.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095723

title: Protecting Your Financial Future: FINRA Investor Advocacy Program
description:
Want to protect your financial future? This presentation is designed to help individuals guard against financial fraud and to make informed decisions before entrusting your money with financial professionals. Participants will leave with a basic understanding of how to spot red flags, an overview of investor rights, and insight into what the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority ("FINRA") is and how it can help investors when problems arise. 

Andrew Marino is a 2026 J.D. Candidate at Seton Hall University School of Law and a Student Advocate for the FINRA Investor Advocacy Program (“IAP”). He is a life-long resident of New Jersey and graduated from Denison University. Mr. Marino has worked as a judicial intern for the District Court of New Jersey, as well as a Summer Associate for a New Jersey firm. Mr. Marino also serves as a Senior Editor for the Seton Hall Law Review. 

 This free, 45-minute presentation is for educational purposes and does not sell or recommend any financial services. The FINRA IAP is one of 10 securities arbitration clinics throughout the United States. The FINRA IAP program at Seton Hall is a pro bono program, which provides free legal representation to New Jersey residents of modest means with securities fraud claims. The IAP was established by a grant from FINRA.

title: Home Repair Essentials NEW
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register. 

Learn the fundamentals of home repair in this practical, hands-on workshop taught by Robert Haas, instructor of the popular Woodworking 101 and Advanced Woodworking classes. Held at Montclair High School, this course gives participants a behind-the-walls look at how common home features are built and maintained.

During the class, Robert will construct two demonstration walls that include doors and a window, showing how these elements are installed and how typical wall structures are assembled. Participants will also learn how traditional double-hung windows operate and how they can be repaired, including basic glass pane replacement.

The workshop will also introduce the basics of residential wiring, demonstrating how outlets and lighting are installed and connected. Throughout the session, Robert will answer questions and offer guidance on a wide range of home repair topics.

Whether you're a homeowner looking to handle basic repairs yourself or simply curious about how things in your home work, this class provides practical knowledge you can use right away.

Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. title: Yom HahShoah Program: After the Holocaust: Family History, Memory, and Identity
description:
**Community Program:**   
7:15PM: Light Meal  
7:40PM - 9:00PM: Program  
  
**After the Holocaust:** **Family History, Memory, and Identity**  
In the aftermath of World War II, Jewish survivors in Displaced Persons (DP) camps rebuilt their lives with remarkable resilience, creating families and ushering in an unprecedented baby boom. At the same time, family memory—preserved through stories, documents, and traditions—became a powerful means of reconstructing identity after profound loss. In this lecture, Dr. Serafima Velkovich explores how family history research and personal narratives intersect with the history of DP camps and demonstrates how individuals can uncover traces of former DP babies within their own family histories.  
  
*Join a conversation with Dr. Sera Velkovich of Yad Vashem and Shirley Grill of Shomrei Emunah as they explore the resilience, and rebuilding of lives in the aftermath of the Shoah.*

title: The Twilight Zone: The Supernatural: Enchanted Objects - Mystical Forces NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Nothing says The Twilight Zone like stories about enchanted objects, mystical forces or the presence of magic. Though they are interesting elements in the stories, the real intrigue lies with how the episodes’ characters behave in their presence. Driven by desperation, curiosity or just plain fear, we get the opportunity to witness lessons learned whether the result is salvation or human downfall.


During five episodes, we will see various responses to the unexplained. Whether they are controlled by superstition, are mishandling seemingly ordinary objects or dismissing what is not fully understood, each character must face the life-changing consequences
of their decisions.


Please join Linda Hetcher this season in the interactive study of the supernatural, most notably presented in The Twilight Zone.

title: [Outside Org]  Aging in Montclair Program committee meeting
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: A Night of Jazz with the Bradford Hayes Quartet
description:
The Bradford Hayes Quartet featuring Bradford Hayes on Saxophone, Michael Cochrane on Piano, Takashi Otsuka on Bass and Gordon Lane on Drums will perform a set of originals and classic jazz hits. 

About the Artist
Bradford Hayes, a native of Dinwiddie County, Virginia has been a force on the jazz scene for many years now. He has performed at a number of clubs, colleges and concert halls that include Birdland (NYC), L'Entrepot (Paris, FRANCE) and the Beacon Theater (NYC). Bradford has performed in different musical settings with Jimmy Heath, Al Grey, Cecil Payne, Rufus Reid, Ray Bryant, Ted Curson, Charles Davis, Joe Lee Wilson, Ben Riley, Dr. Lonnie Smith, Cecil Brooks III, Steve Colson, Kelvin Bell, Pheeroan Aklaff, Michael Cochrane, James Weidman, Nabaté Isles and Donald Braden. Mr. Hayes’ band has also shared the stage with the bands of Gerald Albright, Chico Freeman, Betty Carter, Joe Henderson, Max Roach, Marlena Shaw, Pieces of a Dream, and Hilton Ruiz among others. In addition to leading his own band, Bradford worked with Babatunde Olatunji’s Drums of Passion Band for 15 years until Mr. Olatunji’s passing. Bradford, along with Javon Jackson, Hilton Ruiz, John Lee, and Bill Saxton, was photographed by legendary jazz photographer William Gottlieb in what sadly turned out to be the last jazz photo taken by Mr. Gottlieb. This was the first photo of musicians taken by Mr. Gottlieb since 1948. In addition to this photo session Bradford and the other musicians selected, are also featured as part of a soon to be aired PBS documentary about Mr. Gottlieb. Bradford recently performed a series of concerts in collaboration with the New Jersey Symphony Orchestra as well as a performance with Avant Garde Pianist Steve “Adegoke” Colson at the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

Mr. Hayes holds a Bachelor of Sciences Degree in Music Education from North Carolina A&T State University in Greensboro, North Carolina. He also studied privately with George Coleman and Charles Davis.

www.bradfordhayes.com

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
description:
Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
Jennifer Grim, professor at the University of Miami, will present a masterclass for the Cali flute studio, where students will perform and receive individualized feedback.
Praised by The New York Times as “a deft, smooth flute soloist,” Jennifer Grim is a versatile performer whose work spans solo, chamber, and orchestral stages worldwide.
A member of the flute-percussion duo Solidaire and contemporary ensemble Flagelot, she has collaborated with leading groups including the Boston Chamber Music Society and Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center. Her acclaimed album Through Broken Time reflects her strong commitment to contemporary music.
Grim is Professor and Chair of Instrumental Performance at the Frost School of Music, University of Miami, and serves as President of the National Flute Association Board.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12353914

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Alan Morinis Book Discussion
description:
Join us for a special evening on Thursday, April 16th at 7:30pm with Alan Morinis, acclaimed Mussar teacher and bestselling author, as he discusses his new book, The Shabbat Effect: Jewish Wisdom for Growth and Transformation. In this thoughtful work, Morinis draws on the Mussar tradition to present Shabbat not just as a day of rest, but as a weekly spiritual practice. He explores how stepping back from productivity and control can help us cultivate presence, gratitude, humility, and deeper connection—to ourselves, to others, and to God. Morinis will be interviewed by Rabbi Marc Katz in a dynamic conversation about reclaiming Shabbat’s transformative power in modern life. 
Click here to register!

description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: [MILL Senior Friday] AARP: Spotting Scams & Staying Safe
description:
Join us on Fridays for a day full of events for Seniors with the MILL @ MPL! All events are free and open to the public. Registration is recommended unless otherwise noted.


Have you ever been approached with a fraudulent offer or been a victim of identity theft? These days, scammers are sophisticated. And separating the real from the phony can be tough--especially when emotions are running high. Join us for a free event where we'll take a look into the latest scams, how to avoid them and what to do if you've been targeted. 

We'll explore:


	Common scams, and how to spot and avoid them
	Tactics scammers use to target individuals
	What to do if you're asked for personal information
	Resources to help protect yourself and your family


HOW TO REGISTER
This class is run by AARP. Please register directly through their website here.

For questions about MILL classes please call 973-744-3278.

MILL @ MPL is made possible through a partnership between the MILL, Montclair Public Library and Partners for Health.

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.
 
Join us for the closing reception to meet and connect with the artists, explore the diverse range of work on view, and celebrate the achievements of this dynamic graduating class. Family, friends, faculty, and the public are warmly invited.
 
Mark your calendar and don’t miss this show!
 
BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
Exhibition dates: April 9 – April 20, 2026
Closing Reception: April 20 from 1:00–5:00pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
Light refreshments will be served 

Hosted by: Art and Design

description:

Small Lives in Big Cities film series; Me, Natalie (1969) —Brooklyn NY.

ABOUT THE INSTRUCTOR
MARK PHELAN - I am a lifelong film fanatic who eats, breathes, sleeps and drinks movies. I have studied with Donald Spoto at the New School in NYC and have a BA and MA in Speech and Theatre from MSU. I have also worked as an actor and have directed several plays in local regional and community theaters, including the Whole Theatre in Montclair and the Paper Mill Playhouse in Millburn, NJ. I am currently working as an adjunct professor teaching, among other courses, Introduction to Cinema.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark
description:
Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark
Willie Clark, professor of tuba at University of Maryland and former tuba with the United States AIr Force Band will lead Cali brass students in a masterclass focusing on the fundamentals of expressive brass playing across a landscape of genres.
Bio
Willie Clark is a former member of the United States Air Force Ceremonial Brass and a founding member of the professional Disney tuba quartet, The Tubafours. An internationally active performer, recording artist, and clinician, he has toured extensively throughout the United States, South America, Asia, Australia, and Europe, performing with leading orchestras, ensembles, and popular artists. Clark currently serves on the tuba faculty at the University of Maryland and Howard University, and is widely respected for his engaging teaching and expressive approach to brass performance.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Erev Shabbat services begin on Friday at 6:30pm. These services are especially musical, often with a band of congregants accompanying the Cantor. Usually around an hour, these services are filled with a lot of spirit. It’s not uncommon to see people dancing in their seat or hear the whole congregation clapping their hands. title: 5K Training - No Boundaries
location: Montclair - Anderson Park, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class.

Click here to register for the early bird price, available until April 3rd.

Click here for registrations April 3rd and later.

Have you always wanted to run or walk a 5K but didn't know how to begin training? Maybe you are nervous or don't want to do it alone? Maybe you have had a hard time sticking to a training program in the past? If you answered "Yes" to any of the above, our iconic beginner program is perfect for you. Often the first step is the hardest.

The camaraderie of a group and experience of your coaches and mentors provide great support toward healthier habits and lifestyle. This program is designed for people just starting a walking/running routine for the first time, restarting after a winter hiatus and those who want to run faster or longer. In 8 weeks, meeting twice a week, our fun, unintimidating program will help you walk, walk-run or run across the finish line at the Fitzgerald 5k Lager Run in June.

In collaboration with Dawn and John Fabbro of Fleet Feet Montclair

description:

Saturdays, 9:30 AM – 10:30 AM 
April 18th 
May 9th 
June 13th 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. Each class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. title: Botanical Drawing Workshop
location: Montclair - Cornerstone Montclair, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Welcome spring by immersing yourself in the captivating world of botanical artistry. This all-day workshop welcomes participants of all skill levels and takes place in a supportive, encouraging environment where creativity can truly flourish.

Explore a new way to hone your observational skills as you discover the magic of translating nature’s beauty onto paper using regular and watercolor colored pencils.

Demonstrations will guide how to capture details ranging from the delicate curves of petals to instruction on how to achieve color accuracy. Constructive guidance to help you refine your technique towards developing your individual artistic style will also be given.

Note: Art supplies are not included and must be purchased in advance of this workshop. Materials should cost approximately $80. Visit the link below to view the list of materials needed for this workshop. 
https://www.heidisandecki.com/art-supplies-botanical-drawing-workshop

title: EARTH WEEK CELEBRATION 2026
description:
The Northeast Earth Coalition (NEEC) invites you to join the 2026 Earth Week Celebration April 18-26, for a week of events on the theme of “OUR POWER OUR PLANET.” Between Saturday April 18 and Sunday April 26, we will have volunteer opportunities for cleanups in parks, public spaces, and community gardens. 
The Northeast Earth Coalition is partnering with the Township of Montclair, the Parks and Recreation Committee, Friends of Montclair Parks, and The Brookdale Park Conservancy. 
 
  
THE NEEC IS PART OF THE GREAT GLOBAL CLEANUP® MOVEMENT!
MONTCLAIR EARTH DAY EVENTS: 
APRIL 18, 10-noon:  

Open House at Pine Street Community Garden – come check out the garden and learn about the new solar-powered Rocket composter

APRIL 22, 7-9 pm: 

Movie screening: “The Little Things That Run the World” – part of the Bee City USA series.
Guild Room, First Congregational Church, Montclair

APRIL 25, 10-11:30 am: 

Crane Park Pollinator Garden clean up – help us get the garden ready for Spring, and see what’s already growing! 

APRIL 26, 12-4 pm:

Montclair Earth Fest
South Park St. between Bloomfield Ave. and Church St.


 
For additional information & volunteer opportunities, please e-mail us at: Info@neearth.org or check our calendar for other events and workshops. 

Make every day Earth Day

Fight climate change with diet change
Plant native plants
Stand against deforestation
Organize community cleanups
Compost
Reduce plastic use
Go pesticide free
Turn off lights when not in use
Save paper – switch to online billing
Support organic agriculture

description:

title: Where Do We Go From Here: Chaos or Community? Film event and discussion 7:30 at FCC
description:
Join us in a conversation for our time. Foremost Productions will present “A Community Cultural Event Honoring the Legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. and Dr. Maya Angelou” at the First Congregational Church of Montclair. 

The evening will feature a screening of the award-winning documentary film “Maya Angelou – Reflections of a Blessed Soul,” followed by a special talkback discussion to include the film’s director Hafiz Farid and executive producer Shelley Grodner Seidenstein.

Inspired by Dr. King’s timeless question, “Where Do We Go From Here: Chaos or Community?”, the evening invites audiences to explore the relevance of the words and wisdom of both Dr.King and Dr. Angelou in today’s world. 

The event is free and open to the public, made possible through the support of community sponsors who believe in the importance of accessible cultural programming.

“We can learn to see each other and see ourselves in each other and recognize that human beings are more alike than we are unalike.” -Maya Angelou

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

We occasionally use photos of events in promotional materials and social media. By attending the event, I consent to photos of me being used in promotional materials or on social media.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Passaic Watershed Mountain and Valley Hike
description:
Metrotrails: NJ, PA, NY Metropolitan Area Hiking and More!
Join us for a very happy loop hike, more in our Passaic River series, but this time filling in some gaps of interesting places we missed on previous trips, and featuring Garrett Mountain and Rifle Camp Park this time!
We will meet at 9 am at West Falls Plaza, which we've used a couple of times, and make our way to the Passaic River. This time, we'll cross the river and follow a road upstream on the opposite side we did last time.
We'll get off of the road and walk through Laurel Grove and Mt Nebo Cemeteries, and then Holy Sepulchre Cemetery after a few blocks of road walk.
We will then take the West Side Park trails and Riverside Terrace to the east for a bit. This will lead us around the Great Falls again covering a couple of different trails from before. We then come up past the Reservoir, cross Rt 80 at New Street, and head up into Garrett Mountain Park. We'll make our way through there, and loop in a roundabout way through that park south to Rifle Camp Park. We'll loop from the east side of that as far south as we can, then back to the north on the west side. This will then take us back to Garrett Mountain, and we'll descend west though Zekeriah Park.
A few blocks on the road takes us through Diane Grimes Park, then a bit more to the Morris Canal Greenway. We will loop back to where we started from here to close this very diverse 15 mile loop.
If needed, contact me at 908 343 8374

title: BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design proudly presents the BFA Illustration Thesis Exhibition, a vibrant celebration of the talent, vision, and voices of our graduating seniors. The exhibition will be on view in the Finley Gallery from April 9 through April 20, with a closing reception on Monday, April 20 from 1:00 to 5:00pm.
 
Titled AI: Actual Illustrators, the exhibition responds to the growing presence of artificial image-making by reaffirming the power of human creativity. From paintings, comics, and doodles to animations, visual development, and editorial illustration, each piece reflects the passion, skill, and individuality that can only come from the artist’s hand. This year’s exhibition features the work of 54 emerging artists pushing the boundaries of contemporary illustration. 
 
Participating artists include Alex Allen-Walden, Hailey Amanna, Alison Ays, Amelie Barritas, Arianna Colon, Joe Corredor, Haley Feimster, Adrian Gonzales, Sam Herrera, Cameron Hill, Kayla Ianos, Abubacar Keita, Gavin Kisla, Jerusa Larancuent, Sophie Mahoney, Justine Massabny, Geraldine Medina Rodriguez, Oliver Nazzaro, Katherine Nicholas, Celeste Nova, Sam Olivo, Ashley Ortiz, Yuliya Pasechko, Priya Patel, Amber Paulino, Ayden Pavese, Kimberly Peralta, Perri Polyviou, Ethan Pozo, Nate Quintero, Abigail Reina, Angel Rodriguez, Liany Sanchez, Cyan Villar, Tiana Yurechko, Kaden Zofrea, Kelsey Ayala, Mel Aydogan, Yooyoung Chung, Emily Correa, Alexa D'Alessandro, Elias Galdamez, Jazmin Gamboa, Jayden Gentles, Brianna Ginart, Mee Hee Lim, Pauline Magos, Arianny Matos, Nic Morales, Odosa Obasuyi, Camila Pena-Marte, Elizabeth Seidemann, Jelena Wall, and Jailynn Winn.

title: Lakota Wolf Preserve
description:
We will enjoy a ½ mile nature walk in Columbia, NJ to a preserve, where we will be invited into an observation area surrounded by 4 packs of wolves! We will also get the opportunity to observe Lakota’s foxes, bobcat, and lynx!
How do I sign-up?

Registration opens on Fusion March 1st
Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students $10; Faculty/Staff $20

When and where?

Sunday, April 19, 2026
12:00 pm - 5:00 pm
Begins at Student Recreation Center

What to bring: Water, lunch, long pants, sneakers, and sunscreen.
Potential Hazards: Unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, allergic reactions/poisonous plants, insect bites and injury by running, including sprains, lacerations, abrasions and fractures. 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12179794

title: Interfaith Climate Action Committee Movie Night
Make It Rhyme: Empowering Young Poets (Recommended Grades 3-5)
description: A weekly introduction to poetry covering haiku, sonnet, blank & free verse, are covered in depth. Kids will learn historical significance, structure, and grammatical conventions. We'll write our own poetry and express ourselves in new ways. With excerpts from The Odyssey, Ozymandias, Jabberwocky and more serving as writing inspiration. Kids will also make art based on their poems, further demonstrating their creativity. Facilitated by poet Jack Jerow. Registration recommended for reminders.

title: Second Sunday Concert - Cart & Marrazza (rescheduled date)
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Rescheduled from December, 2025 due to snowstorm. The previous reservations for this performance will be honored. Unclaimed seats will be available right before the start of the concert. There is no waitlist. 
  
Now April 19, 2026 – Marrazza-Cart Duo 
HOPE WILL NEVER BE SILENT 
Robert Cart, flutist, and Regina DiMedio-Marrazza, piano, are a chamber ensemble, renowned for
passionate and musically stirring performances throughout the United States, including concerts at Carnegie Hall, the Kennedy Center, the Philadelphia Ethical Society, the Philadelphia International Music Festival, the National Flute Association, and several colleges and universities. The duo has premiered new works by Jennifer Higdon and Gary Schocker. Advocates of new music, they have also premiered new works by Louis Barroso, Jennifer Higdon, and Gary Schocker. They have graced our stage at Van Vleck House many times and are a series favorite with concert-goers. 
All Second Sunday concerts begin at 2PM. Seats are limited and reservations are recommended. If registered guests do not attend, those seats will be open to visitors right before the start of the concert.

title: Mah Jongg Meetup: Spring Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following meetups.

The Mah Jongg Meetup provides a weekly opportunity for players to continue practicing their established skills, sharpen their strategies, build confidence at the game table, and socialize with fellow Mah Jongg players!

The library will provide five reserved tables in the cafe! 

NOTE: Attendees should bring their personal sets to this meetup and should already have an understanding of the Mah Jongg game. Attendees should bring their 2025 or 2026 National Mah Jongg League card to the meetup. Bringing an additional set is encouraged!

*Note: There will be a limited number of Mah Jongg sets available on a first-come, first-served basis.

If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here.

 

IMPORTANT REMINDERS:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 
	Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space - Weekly Monday Morning - NEW
description:
The Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space provides a weekly opportunity for teaching yourself the basics of American Mah Jongg at your own pace.

The library will provide the book Beginner's Guide to American Mah Jongg by Elaine Sandberg, Mah Jongg card, a set and table!  You can also boost your learning with a list of  Mah Jongg resources available at the library. 

After you know the basics of Mah Jongg, and would like to play the game with other Mah Jongg players, please register for the "Mah Jongg Meetup" by clicking here.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond.
All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

Hosted by: Advocacy and Outreach

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/11332856

You know about the American Revolution, but how much do you really know about the tyrant who drove them to revolution?The History Club invites students to a presentation exploring the American Revolution through the perspective of King George III. This event will examine who King George really was, how he viewed the colonies, and how his decisions shaped the course of the Revolution.

Hosted by: The Historical Society at Montclair State

title: Book a Reading Specialist (parents/educators of grades PreK-12)
location: Montclair Main - Children's Floor, Montclair Public Library
description:
Did you know Montclair Public Library Youth Services has a full-time Reading Specialist?

Gina Chung Fortt has 14 years of experience in the Elementary grades as a classroom teacher and pull-out Reading Specialist. Gina is super passionate about supporting struggling readers and empowering parents and caregivers to guide their child(ren)'s literacy journey.

Please sign up for a 30-minute consultation with our Youth Services Reading Specialist. Some topics may include:


	How to support your child's reading at home
	Review your child's current reading assessment (please bring copies, if possible)
	Learn what resources we have at the library to check out for your developing or struggling reader
	Other areas of concern or curiosity regarding your child's literacy development


Sign up HERE for a slot!

Adults only. Registration is required.

title: Garden to Table - Butterfly Waffles
description:
Garden to Table 
Mondays, 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM 
April 20 – Butterfly Waffles 
When exploring the gardens you are sure to spot monarch butterflies. Join us for our April Garden to Table class to make Butterfly Waffles, and learn more about why monarchs are so important to have in our gardens. 
Enjoy quality time in the garden with your child/children as we explore the Edible Garden and all that it has to offer. Help us weed and water, plant, and harvest. We will prepare a simple snack with ingredients inspired by the garden. Appropriate for children ages 4 and up but all are welcome. Children must be accompanied by an adult. 
Sponsored by  
Fee: $15/class per child for Members / $20/class per child for General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class

title: Basic Italian 1B - Online
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

It's time to brush up on your Italian!  Basic Italian 1B is for students who have taken Basic Italian I or Italian for Travelers and would like to communicate more effectively in handling transactions, making reservations, ordering in a restaurant, asking directions…They will learn how to express themselves in talking about the present and the past.  If you speak another Romance language or need a quick review, this is a good beginning for your study of Italian. 


Prerequisite: Basic Italian 1, Italian for Travelers or equivalent.

title: Mah Jongg - Tournament-Style Play NEW
location: Montclair Bellevue Ave Branch - Kids Place, Montclair Bellevue Avenue Branch
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Learn how to master the speed and strategy of Mah Jongg for tournaments.

After years of playing in casual Mah Jongg games, more experienced players may be looking to challenge themselves in tournament play but feel intimidated by the idea.  Are you good enough?  What is a "table" rule versus a NMJL rule? The trick to tournament play isn't just skill, but is the confidence in that skill and your knowledge of NMJL rules. This class will clarify those rules and give tips to make you confident in your play.

Note: Students must bring their own 2026 National Mah Jongg League hand card to class. The Bellevue Avenue Branch is NOT wheelchair accessible.

title: Practical Spanish 1
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is for those of you who know little to no Spanish and are interested in acquiring some practical communication skills. You will be introduced to basic greetings and practice useful questions and answers. You will learn the fundamentals of Spanish pronunciation while mastering introductory grammar and vocabulary.
 
Topics covered:


	conversation/questions
	greetings
	personal information
	expressions of courtesy
	feelings
	asking for direction
	talk about needs and wants
	where people are from
	Alphabet
	Numbers/days/date
	Colors
	Basic commands
	Some ar/er/ir  regular verbs
	Verb to be: ser/estar
	Verb to go: ir
	Verbs: tener/necesitar/querer
	Ir   a
	Negation
	Subject pronouns/difference between tú/usted
	Gender and number of nouns/definite/indefinite articles
	Some adjectives including cuánto/a
	The expression “Hay”
	Basic geography, culture and food

title: Italian Conversation - Online
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is appropriate for those who have some basic knowledge of Italian. Each class focuses on the use of Italian and cultural knowledge for communicating in a variety of situations including shopping, sightseeing, making reservations, dining out and having a simple conversation with others.  Students will speak using the present tense and will be introduced to simple grammar to express past time.  We will review many relevant expressions and vocabulary in context. This course will be appropriate for students who have completed Italian for Travelers 1 and 2 or Basic Italian 1 & 2.

title: Practical French 2
location: Montclair Main - Teen Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Practical French 2 is a continuation of French 1. Master additional grammar, more advanced verbs and vocabulary. Conversation is an essential component for those who wish to focus on more speaking and proper pronunciation. Students who are considered advanced beginners are welcome to join the class.

title: Inner Body Meditation (20 minutes)
description:
The Mindful Stream
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body. Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled.
(Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.21

title: THRIVE Conference
description:
On behalf of EngageNJ, we are excited to announce our 7th Annual THRIVE Conference will be hosted at Montclair State University, 7th Floor Conference Center on April 21st from 9:00AM - 3:00 PM!
 
Conference Highlights: 
This conference is FREE to all attendees! 
Gain insights from professionals and student leaders who are paving the way for the future generation.
Breakfast & lunch are provided. 
 
Click here to register  https://forms.monday.com/forms/adb42295b54232821c8d33cbc5ffee16?r=use1 
 
The deadline to register for this conference is Monday, April 6th.
 
We are looking for presenters! We are seeking workshops centered around professional development, student leadership, or community building. The workshop should be 45 minutes in total. The deadline to submit this form is March 31st.
 
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to perezso@montclair.edu or info@engagenj.org

title: upLIFT
description:
Step into the brand-new PLAE Room and discover upLIFT—a judgement-free program designed to help every student, from complete beginners to seasoned gym-goers, feel confident and capable with free-weight training. Learn proper lifting technique, get personalized form checks, and master the basics of creating your own effective workout programs. Whether you’re looking to meet other students who love to lift, pursue new fitness goals, or simply feel more at home in the weight room, upLIFT offers a supportive community and a dedicated space to grow. The program is structured for steady progression throughout the semester, but you’re welcome to drop in anytime and join at your own pace.

Registration is required as only one staff member is available to assist at this time. 20-25 minutes are allotted per patron. If registrations are full, please check a future date, as we cannot accommodate walk-ins at this time.

Please note that some technological issues may be above the ability of the library staff and you may be referred out to a better suited tech professional. These drop-ins are not professional IT services, we cannot fix computers or smartphones.

title: Savory and Sweet Arab Cuisine: Fattoush Salad and Jallab NEW
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.



In another edition of FARAH’s Sweet & Savory Flavors of Arab Cuisine series at MPL, discover the aromatic spices of Arab cuisine. Join Lilianne Gooch for a guided cooking demonstration on how to make two classic dishes with an emphasis on spices: one sweet and one savory. 
 



Fattoush: The Vibrant Taste of the Levant

The perfect chopped salad, Fattoush is a refreshing, crunchy, and colorful staple in Arab cuisine featuring watercress or romaine lettuce, tomatoes, cucumber, radishes, and fresh herbs, all tossed in a tangy signature lemon-sumac-pomegranate molasses vinaigrette. Added crunch and flavor comes from crispy toasted pita chips topped with za’atar. Join us to learn about the unique spices that make up this flavorful Arab salad.  
 



Jallab: The Essence of Arab Hospitality

Transport your senses to the Levant with this traditional and refreshing drink. Learn how to mix date, grape and carob molasses with rose water into a fragrant non-alcoholic drink. Perfect for warm days, it is traditionally served chilled and topped with pine nuts and raisins. This is the quintessential Arab mocktail.

title: Writing: Method & Motivation
location: Montclair Main - Teen Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

A workshop for anyone who wants to write, or wants to infuse their existing writing practice with new energy. In each class, we'll consider one element of fiction or creative non-fiction, such as point of view, narrative voice, and what makes a piece of writing riveting, or boring. Students can expect in-class writing prompts, reading aloud, and optional homework. Positive encouragement, rather than formal critiques, will be featured, and inspiration and organization for writers will be discussed. Students should bring whatever they use to write to each class.

title: Creating a Compelling College Application (High School Juniors & Families)
description:
This is an online presentation; you must sign up here to gain access.
From our friends at Stand Out for College:

We are offering this Zoom presentation to help high school juniors and their parents understand how to use the upcoming summer productively to create a compelling college application.

We will cover the college application process, including current admissions trends, writing the personal statement and supplemental essays, securing strong teacher recommendations, and understanding how students can position themselves to increase their chances of admission.

We will also explore why a student’s ability to express insights, experiences, and personal growth through their essays is more important than ever.

Our presentation will include application strategies, such as the advantages and disadvantages of early decision, early action, and regular decision. We will review the factors students should consider when deciding whether to submit standardized test scores for test optional colleges and universities. We will also explain the different pathways through which students can be admitted to a particular school.

We will examine the holistic admissions review process, what admissions officers consider important when evaluating applicants, and how students can develop a compelling narrative that strengthens their applications.

We will also discuss the importance of community service and how it offers opportunities for personal growth and character development. Admissions officers place significant emphasis on a student’s character and the ways they have contributed to their communities.

This program provides a detailed and strategic overview of what students should focus on to make their college applications as compelling as possible.

The presentation will be led by Ron Feuchs and Jackie Tepper, partners at Stand Out For College, LLC (www.standoutforcollege.com).

title: 🌎EARTH DAY clean up at Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
**This is a special event for Earth Day 2026. We will be collecting trash along our usual hiking route. Trash bags and disposable gloves will be provided**

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095724

title: Wine Confidence 101: Understanding the Basics
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This introductory course offers a comprehensive overview of essential wine concepts, designed to establish or strengthen your foundational knowledge and support your ongoing appreciation of wine. 

Over the 5-week course, we will review the primary grape varieties and styles of dry white, rosé, red, and sparkling wines, as well as explore viticulture fundamentals and the winemaking process. We will explore the major wine-producing countries and differences among grape varieties and wine styles. We will review how to taste and critically evaluate wine from visual assessment through aftertaste. For historical context, the course provides an overview of wine’s origins, its evolution, and the business aspects of winemaking. A solid foundation also includes guidance on appropriate tools for opening wine bottles, optimal storage, and serving temperatures to enhance your overall wine experience. 

By the end of the 5-week class, you will have gained a deeper understanding of the many aspects of dry and sparkling wines so you are thoroughly conversant about wine and have tasted between 20 – 25 different wines from around the world! 

THIS EVENT IS 21  Students will be required to show a government ID to the instructor upon arriving at the first session.

title: Home Repair Essentials NEW
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register. 

Learn the fundamentals of home repair in this practical, hands-on workshop taught by Robert Haas, instructor of the popular Woodworking 101 and Advanced Woodworking classes. Held at Montclair High School, this course gives participants a behind-the-walls look at how common home features are built and maintained.

During the class, Robert will construct two demonstration walls that include doors and a window, showing how these elements are installed and how typical wall structures are assembled. Participants will also learn how traditional double-hung windows operate and how they can be repaired, including basic glass pane replacement.

The workshop will also introduce the basics of residential wiring, demonstrating how outlets and lighting are installed and connected. Throughout the session, Robert will answer questions and offer guidance on a wide range of home repair topics.

Whether you're a homeowner looking to handle basic repairs yourself or simply curious about how things in your home work, this class provides practical knowledge you can use right away.

title: Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn
description:
Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn
Dr. Jason Abbott, Professor of Political Science and International Relations at Montclair State University, will deliver a public lecture as part of the Lectures on Tap series in Brooklyn, New York.
 
Title: Godzilla: Japan, Trauma, and the Unkillable MonsterDate: Wednesday, April 22, 2026Time: 6:30 p.m. – 8:30 p.m. (Lecture begins at 7:00 p.m.)Location: Bushwick, Brooklyn, NY (exact address provided upon registration)Please Register: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/lectures-on-tap-godzilla-japan-trauma-and-the-unkillable-monster-tickets-1985135400421?aff=oddtdtcreator
 
Lectures on Tap is a public lecture series that brings professors, experts, and storytellers into informal venues to deliver accessible, thought-provoking talks.
 
In this lecture, Dr. Abbott examines Godzilla as a cultural lens through which to understand Japan’s postwar experience. Emerging in the shadow of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Godzilla has long served as a cinematic reflection of national trauma, nuclear anxiety, and unresolved historical memory. The talk explores why the figure of Godzilla reappears at moments of crisis and what this enduring monster reveals about how societies process catastrophe and construct collective memory.
 
Drawing on examples across multiple films and historical periods, the lecture considers how trauma is not simply resolved, but often reworked and reimagined over time.
Agenda:6:30 p.m. – Doors open; seating, food, and drinks available6:55 p.m. – Host introduction7:00 p.m. – Lecture begins7:45 p.m. – Audience Q&A8:00 p.m. – Informal discussion and networking8:30 p.m. – Event concludes

Hosted by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS)

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12354484

title: Movie screening: "The Little Things That Run the World" - Earth Week Celebration
location: Guild Room at First Congregational Church, 40 South Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, New Jersey, 07042, 07042, United States
description:
Join us for a screening of “The Little Things That Run the World,” part of our Bee City USA program. 
Synopsis:
Life on Earth is in the middle of a major re-alignment of species composition. The smallest of creatures – insects – hold the fabric of all life together. They are the most numerous of all animal groups on the planet. But insects are declining at an alarming rate throughout the world, threatening innumerable species, whole ecosystems, and even our food systems with collapse. This is story about untangling the mysteries as to why something so ubiquitous – and seemingly endless – could be on the cusp of disappearing entirely. It is also the story of heroic efforts to save these most vulnerable of creatures. 
Admission is free. Register to save your seat. 
Learn more about the film here. 
*The movie will begin promptly at 7 pm; please arrive by 6:45.

title: The Twilight Zone: The Supernatural: Enchanted Objects - Mystical Forces NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Nothing says The Twilight Zone like stories about enchanted objects, mystical forces or the presence of magic. Though they are interesting elements in the stories, the real intrigue lies with how the episodes’ characters behave in their presence. Driven by desperation, curiosity or just plain fear, we get the opportunity to witness lessons learned whether the result is salvation or human downfall.


During five episodes, we will see various responses to the unexplained. Whether they are controlled by superstition, are mishandling seemingly ordinary objects or dismissing what is not fully understood, each character must face the life-changing consequences
of their decisions.


Please join Linda Hetcher this season in the interactive study of the supernatural, most notably presented in The Twilight Zone.

title: Agentic AI Finance: Governed Metrics, Open Access, Automated Action on Databrick
description:
Cloud Data Driven
Large finance organizations already rely on dashboards and reports, but the real challenge is enabling automation without breaking governance or auditability. This session shares how a global finance organization implemented agent-driven workflows on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform using Unity Catalog, metrics views, and open lakehouse patterns.

The talk walks through a real implementation where finance metrics are defined once using metrics views, protected with attribute-based access control and automated classification, and reused consistently across reporting, analytics, and automation. Power BI connects directly to governed lakehouse tables and metrics for enterprise reporting, while Iceberg-compliant clients consume Delta tables through Uniform, enabling open access without duplicating data.

Agentic workflows observe certified metrics and events, assist with variance investigation, anomaly triage, and operational follow-ups, and propose actions that remain traceable and reviewable. Early results indicated several hours of manual investigation effort saved per reporting cycle and faster anomaly triage, without introducing additional governance or audit risk. The session includes an architecture walkthrough and demo using synthetic data, reflecting real finance constraints, trade-offs, and lessons learned when moving from reporting to governed, open, and action-oriented analytics.

title: MILL Botanicals Watercolor Workshop
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
These classes are a Partner Program with the Montclair Institute for Lifelong Learning. To register please visit: http://schedulesplus.com/montclair/kiosk 
Thursdays, 1:30 PM – 2:30 PM 
April 23 – Spring Botanicals Watercolor Workshop – Instructor: Karen DeLuca: Learn the art of painting flowers and leaves in watercolors. Bring your own paints/brushes to class but we will have some to loan. 
May 21 – What’s in Bloom Tour – Join Laura Roberts, Director of Horticulture at Van Vleck, for a journey through the beauty of the season. Discover enchanting details and appreciate majestic specimens. Outdoor tour, so please dress for the weather with appropriate footwear. (Outdoor Program, indoors if inclement weather) 
May 28 – Focus on Flowers Walk – Spend an afternoon in the garden taking a close-up look at the beauty of flowers. After learning about each flower, discover helpful hints in capturing photos using your phone or camera. Instructors: Laura Roberts and Tracey Diamond (Outdoor Program, indoors if inclement weather) 
June 4 – Birds of Prey Program – Flat Rock Brook Nature Center will visit Van Vleck with live birds of prey. Learn fascinating facts about these incredible creatures that live right here in New Jersey. (Indoor Program) 
June 11 – Late Spring Blooming Tree tour – This guided tree identification walk led by Laura Roberts will focus on spring-blooming trees. This is an outdoor tour, so please dress for the weather with appropriate footwear. (Outdoor Program, indoors if inclement weather) 
June 18 – Birds and Art – We’ll learn about some of our favorite resident birds at Van Vleck with Naturalist Jenny Aley and discuss their summer activities. We will also explore our creative side with a simple painting or sketching activity that will help us identify some of our beautiful native birds. No experience necessary – all levels of birders and artists welcome! (Indoor Program) 
June 25th – Mindfulness in the Garden – Jodi Brooks will guide us through very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Cold or rainy weather – class will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). (Outdoor Program, indoors if inclement weather)
