title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

Hosted by: Cru

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12176635

title: Shabbat Your Way
description:
Whether in your own home or someone else’s, with one person or many, having an elaborate meal or ordering take-out, there is not one way to celebrate Shabbat. “Shabbat Your Way” is an opportunity to cultivate a Shabbat mindset in a way that feels right for you. Shomrei will provide resources and ideas to help you add something new or special to your Shabbat practice or gathering.

title: Free Family Swim Night
description:
Campus Recreation invites you to bring your family (up to 4 guests) to Free Family Swim Night on Friday, April 24 from 5:00-9:30 p.m. at the Student Recreation Center Pool. You and your family will have FREE use of our pool and locker rooms (no membership to the facility is required). Flotation devices are allowed as long as they are USCG approved. We have life jackets available - just ask the lifeguard.
 
Please note the following:

Bring your Montclair ID in order to check-in.
No prior registration required.
You must accompany your family or guest(s) at all times during the program.
All children must be over the age of 5 and fully potty-trained.

When and Where?

Saturday, April 24, 2026
5:00 pm - 9:45 pm
Student Recreation Center - Pool 

Email us at srcaquatics@montclair.edu with any questions.  We look forward to seeing you at the pool!

title: Writing as Spiritual Practice Covenant Group
description:
Are you interested in exploring the monthly themes through writing and sharing with others? The purpose of this group is to write about the monthly theme before the meeting and share your writing with the group. Facilitated by Dennis Kurtti, it will be held each month on the fourth Saturday. This is not a writing critique group, and all levels of writing experience are welcome. If you would like to understand more about this kind of covenant group, you can watch the worship service held on 8/21/22, which featured speakers from an already existing group. To express your interest in this group, contact Laura Wilson at CovenantGroups@uumontclair.org

title: Inner Body Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body. Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled.

(Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.

title: Casual Volleyball - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
We're a group of people who will start by hitting the ball around for the first 20-30 minutes and then we'll break up into 2-4 teams for two or three hours of play.

If we have more than 20 people show up, we'll break up into three or four teams of 6 (Some may have 7, 8 or 9. Additional players just rotate into play). Games will be to 10 points. Rally scoring. Winner stays on (unless a team has won four straight).

We'll be heading outdoors to the hard court in Leonia off Overpeck County Pk Driveway in the Overpeck County Park.

\*\*\* After playing, we'll be likely heading to Tropical Juice Bar, an idea of regular attendee Kate, for a drink and snack. It's located at 60 River St. in Hackensack. Feel free to join us or suggest another spot for a post-volleyball hangout. \*\*\*

There's no fee to play volleyball and nothing else you need to do besides show up. Come anytime!! All are welcome!!! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form. We're more for people who haven't played in months, maybe years, and just want to occasionally play again and hang out.

Directions: You can Google "Volleyball Court in Leonia" and it should come up. Or type 41 Willow Tree Road, Leonia, NJ 07605 into your GPS, but note that it's not on Willow Tree Road, just near it. Instead, turn onto Overpeck County Pk Driveway off Fort Lee Road (Degraw Avenue). It is past the tennis courts and near a basketball court and track. You can try Googling Leonia High School as well. That should help you find it.

We don't have the court reserved and if it happens to be taken, we may play with those already occupying it or we will try to relocate on the fly. It's certainly possible that we may have to cancel that day if we can't find a suitable spot. Please keep that in mind. And if it's looking like it'll rain or snow, I'll make a call on whether to cancel around an hour ahead of time.

Please bring a ball and net if you have them for possible use during the game. Feel free to bring a speaker for music, or snacks and drinks for consumption during play.

If you have trouble finding the court or group, you can call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609. There is plenty of parking and an open bathroom at the park.

Hope you'll join us for our 18th volleyball event. Questions or comments? You can message Andy on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.

Check out bowling, soccer, volleyball, tennis or one of our other activities at: www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Jewish without Apology: A Conversation with Sarah Hurwitz
description:
**Seth Goodchild Memorial Lecture**  
Reception immediately following  
  
RSVP: http://shomrei.org/hurwitz  
  
**About the Program**  
Award-winning author Sarah Hurwitz joins Rabbi Julie Roth for a candid conversation about rediscovering Judaism as an adult and why its intellectual depth and moral clarity feel strikingly relevant to modern life. From finding meaning in a chaotic world to standing tall against rising antisemitism, Sarah explores why embracing Jewish wisdom isn’t just about honoring the past -- it’s about sanctifying the present and elevating our shared humanity.   
  
**Free Babysitting Available**  
Email Aviva Arad, aarad@shomrei.org  
  
**\*Additional parking is available next door at 73 & 77 Park Street.**  
  
**In Loving Memory of Seth Goodchild**   
Seth Goodchild was inspired by his family to convert to Judaism at the end of his life. To honor his legacy and share his love of Judaism, his friends and family sponsored this lecture and a class on exploring Judaism.

title: Miles Without Borders
description:
Miles Without Borders is a high-energy, Olympic-style field day bringing together fun, fitness, and philanthropy! In collaboration with MSU Run for Fun, Friends of Doctors Without Borders (FOMSF) invites students to compete in exciting team-based challenges, races, and mini-games designed for all skill levels.
This event isn’t just about winning—it’s about making a difference. As part of our initiative, we are hosting a food drive to support local communities in need. Participants and attendees are encouraged to bring non-perishable food items to contribute to the cause.
Come out to run, play, and give back while supporting global medical humanitarian efforts. Whether you're competing or cheering from the sidelines, your participation helps make an impact both locally and worldwide.
Let’s move with purpose and turn friendly competition into meaningful change!

Hosted by: Friends of Doctors Without Borders, MSU Run For Fun

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available courtesy of our host! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
*
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.
source: Meetup: Board Game Night at Verona Inn

Early Spring Session: Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Winter session will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee: 3 April classes: General public $75, Members $60 
Series registration only. Pre Registration is required – there are no drop-ins for this class. 
 
Late Spring Session: May 5, 12 19 & 26 
Early Summer Session: June 2, 9, 16 & 23

title: Denim Day 2026: Mind the Gaps, Move Forward
description:
April is Sexual Assault Awareness Month, and together, we can break the silence. The Montclair State University Police Department and the Office of Advocacy & Outreach are committed to fostering a campus where survivors feel heard, supported, and empowered. It starts with believing. It starts with you. By offering a safe and affirming response to those who disclose gender-based and sexual violence, we can create lasting change. Let’s stand together to end the cycle of silence—because every voice matters, and every action counts.
Denim Day is recognized internationally as a day of action and awareness, encouraging people to wear denim to challenge victim-blaming and educate others about sexual violence. This year, Denim Day falls on Wednesday, April 29, 2026.
The University Police and Office of Advocacy & Outreach and the Office of Advocacy and Community Resilience, in partnership with over 30 campus and local community organizations, invites you to join us from 9 a.m. to 12 p.m. at the Amphitheater for a powerful awareness event honoring survivors and increasing awareness of available resources.
Despite the gaps that still exist in the criminal justice system, in resources, and in prevention education, we will move forward together as a community. As part of this commitment, we will walk one mile around campus in solidarity with survivors.

Hosted by: Advocacy and Outreach

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/11871313

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Implementing Zero Trust Security in Modern Enterprise Data Architectures​
description:
Cloud Data Driven
As enterprise data ecosystems expand across hybrid and multi-cloud environments, traditional perimeter-based security models are no longer sufficient to protect sensitive information. Organizations are increasingly adopting Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) to secure complex data environments where information flows across distributed platforms, services, and organizational boundaries.

This session explores how Zero Trust principles can be applied to modern enterprise data architectures, with a focus on the security implications of federated and centralized data models. The discussion examines how these architectures approach identity management, access control, and data governance within a Zero Trust framework. Federated architectures offer advantages in data sovereignty, localized policy enforcement, and distributed resilience, while centralized architectures enable unified security policies, centralized monitoring, and coordinated threat response.

The session will also highlight how AI-driven security capabilities are transforming Zero Trust implementations by enabling real-time threat detection, behavioral analytics, and automated response mechanisms. Additionally, the talk will address strategies for protecting AI-powered data pipelines, maintaining data lineage visibility, and ensuring compliance with evolving regulatory requirements.

Attendees will gain practical insights into designing secure, scalable enterprise data systems, understanding the trade-offs between federated and centralized models, and implementing Zero Trust strategies that strengthen data protection in AI-enabled environments.

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: New Jersey Restaurant & Hospitality Association: ServSafe Food Safety Manager Course & Exam
description: ServSafe Food Safety Manager Training and Certification is required by law in the state of New Jersey. Currently, the state law requires that at least one person per establishment be ServSafe Food Safety Manager-certified. Classes are taught in one day—you take both the class and the exam in the same day.The class will run from 9am to 5pm with a break for lunch. Be sure to arrive on time with a #2 pencil and a photo ID. You will need a 70% or above in order to pass the test. Since ServSafe is now offering electronic certificates, please ensure that you provide a valid email address on the answer sheet. You will receive your certificate via email within 2-3 weeks. Some important notes: You must be logged into your NJRHA member portal to receive the member discount. Please contact saritac@njrha.org or info@njrha.org if you need help accessing the portal or don't know your login credentials.The "promo code" field on the NJRHA registration page is how they allow you to pay to retest. title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: CSAM Science Teaching Program - Program Meeting
description:
The CSAM Science Teaching Program holds biweekly meetings that are open, welcoming, and inclusive! Any student curious about science teaching is invited to join us. Each meeting highlights a theme related to science education, from classroom strategies to career pathways, and offers students opportunities to connect with mentors, explore internships, and gain real-world experience working with teachers and children. Students are encouraged to bring a guest who may also be interested in exploring science teaching.
These gatherings help us build a strong community of support, where students encourage one another, share experiences, and access valuable opportunities such as scholarships, paid internships, and mentoring from advanced peers and in-service science teachers. Whether you are just beginning to explore the idea of teaching or already on the path toward certification, these meetings are a place to grow, learn, and belong.

Hosted by: College of Science and Mathematics (CSAM)

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/11712551

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Meditation with John Welshons 
Wednesday, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
May 6, 13, 20 
June 3, 10, 24 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher. 
Important Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: NJ Real Estate Broker 90-Hour Basic Broker Course | Burgos Realty Academy
description: Registration Open NJ Real Estate Broker 90-Hour Basic Broker Course (Evening/Weekend) May 7th - June 18th T/W/Th/Sat Burgos Realty Academy offers the 90-hour Basic Broker course as part of New Jersey’s required 150-hour Broker pre-licensing curriculum. This instructor-led course covers advanced real estate law, brokerage practices, contracts, finance, valuation, and regulatory requirements essential for broker licensure and long-term success. The full 150-hour New Jersey Broker pre-licensing requirement includes: 30 Hours – Ethics & Agency 30 Hours – Office Management 90 Hours – Basic Broker Courses are taught by a licensed broker-owner, real estate instructor, and ARELLO Certified Distance Education Instructor. Instruction is designed to prepare students not only to pass the broker exam, but to operate with confidence, compliance, and professional authority in today’s real estate industry.

Join us for an enchanting Cream Tea in the Living Room at Van Vleck House! 
Upon arrival, you will be offered a menu of fine teas and infusions. Your choice of tea will be brewed to perfection. Once everyone is settled in, the tiers will arrive with freshly baked scones, jam, homemade clotted cream, scrumptious sandwiches, and a dessert. We hope you will join us! 
This event is for adults and children ages 13+. For group bookings of more than 8 guests, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. Seatings are 90 minutes, and registration is required. 
Dietary Restrictions
A gluten-free option is available with prior notice. We are not able to accommodate a dairy-free or vegan diet. All requests must be communicated to us 48 hours before the event. You may note this request on the checkout page. For dietary/allergy inquiries, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. 
Cancellation Policy
We are unable to accommodate cancellations or rescheduling. If you find you are no longer able to attend your scheduled event, you may transfer your ticket(s) to another person(s) to attend instead. Please notify Van Vleck House and Gardens so we can update your ticket name(s) for event check-in. 
Please note that this event is a fundraiser, and a portion of your ticket price will go towards supporting our beloved Van Vleck House & Gardens. 
Member Price: $45
General Price: $55
A 10% gratuity for your server is included in tea pricing.

title: SQL Supercharged: Azure, Fabric & Copilot in Action
description:
Cloud Data Driven
Get ready to explore the **next generation of SQL innovation** across Azure SQL, SQL Server, and the brand‑new **SQL in Microsoft Fabric**. We’ll break down what’s changing, why it matters, and how you can start taking advantage of these capabilities immediately.

🔹 **Azure SQL & SQL on Fabric** See how the newest features—**database mirroring, real‑time analytics, and intelligent performance insights**—unlock scenarios that were impossible just a year ago.

🔹 **SQL Server: What’s Coming Next** Preview the upcoming SQL Server release with a focus on **performance enhancements, security improvements, and operational efficiency** that will reshape how teams build and manage data systems.

🔹 **Copilot for Data Professionals** Watch how Copilot transforms the way we **query, manage, and reason about data**, accelerating productivity and simplifying complex tasks across the SQL ecosystem.

If you’re working in data engineering, analytics, or database administration, this session will give you **practical, future‑ready insights** you can apply right away.

**Register now and join us—don’t miss this deep dive into the future of SQL.**

title: Bowling Night - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
This is an event for casual bowling at Montvale Lanes, 14 W. Grand Ave. All are welcome for **two games, food and drinks**, and any post- or pre-game hang out. Show up anytime! Questions or comments? You can message Andy Milone on Meetup, or call/text him at **201-234-9609**.

\*\*\* Before bowling, we might stop by the Pizza Parlour, 6 S. Kinderkamack Road in Montvale, for a few slices. Post a comment on the event page to gauge interest. **After bowling, we'll definitely be heading to Davey's Irish Pub & Restaurant, 5 Park St. in Montvale, or The Ridge Diner, 125 Kinderkamack Road in Park Ridge, at 10 p.m. for some food and/or drinks.** \*\*\*

About the group: People of all ages, backgrounds and abilities are welcome! We're not professionals and we're not in a league. In fact, we're a group of people who probably each won't score more than 100 points and will land more than our fair share of gutter balls. There's no pressure at all!!!! We're not here to analyze your form or technique.

More event details: You pay for your own games, food and drinks. Please bring cash. The alley does not accept card. But there is an ATM on site. **The** **cost per player is $18 for two games** **and bowling shoes.** If you have your own shoes, the cost is $12. Please try to bring exact change since Andy will have a limited amount of cash. If you bring a $20 bill and he doesn't have change, any remainder would just go toward the bowling alley tip.

Food and drinks (alcoholic and non-alcoholic) are available on site, plus tables and an alley bar to hang at during the event. There is a dart board and a few arcade games, as well.

**What do you do when you get there?** First, find the group inside around the front desk. Look for a growing group of people! Call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609 if you can't find us. Once Andy secures the lanes, please pay him before getting your bowling shoes.

**Please know that it is possible we have to wait 20-40 minutes until lane(s) free up.** It's also possible that we may have to relocate or cancel that night if there's a long wait list upon arrival. Please keep that in mind if traveling from far away!

For those who don't have cars or are looking to avoid driving, the Montvale Train Station is only like a five-minute walk from the bowling alley and can be reached via the Pascack Valley Line. Last train leaves at 12:20 a.m. and heads toward Hoboken. (https://gmtma.org/wp-content/uploads/2011/11/rail_system_map.jpg)

Many thanks to Taylor, one of our group participants, for recommending Montvale Lanes! You can learn more at: www.montvalelanes.com.

Hope you'll join us for our 23rd bowling event, or consider attending one of our volleyball, ping pong, skating or other activities -> www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Noontime Cornhole - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
This is an event for two to three hours of casual cornhole at a local park.

The gathering is tentatively scheduled for Lodi Memorial Park, behind the library, 1 Memorial Dr., because the area has four permanent concrete boards.

There is not a ton of parking, but there is a huge lot across the street as part of the shopping plaza.

Comment below if you have your own boards and beanbags, or if you want to bring another yard game. A volleyball court and basketball court, as well as turf field, are also on site. If anyone knows of any other permanent cornhole setups in North Jersey, feel free to also make that suggestion. We may relocate as a result.

\*\*\* After playing, we'll likely be heading somewhere for a drink and snack. Feel free to suggest a spot for a post-cornhole hangout. \*\*\*

There's no fee to play and nothing else you need to do besides show up. Come anytime!! All are welcome!!! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form. We're here for people who haven't played in months, maybe years, and just want to occasionally play and hang out.

The event comes to you courtesy of prior suggestions for future events and our group's WhatsApp chat.

Hope you'll join us for our first cornhole event. Questions or comments? You can message Andy on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.
Check out bowling, soccer, volleyball, tennis, skating or one of our other activities at: [www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball](https://www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball)

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Silent Failures in AI Data Systems: Risk Drift in Pipelines​
description:
Cloud Data Driven
As AI becomes embedded in cloud data platforms, organizations are encountering failure modes that differ fundamentally from traditional deterministic systems. In contrast to binary failures, AI-augmented pipelines often degrade silently, with quality erosion emerging gradually and compounding over time.

This talk explores key failure patterns in production AI data systems. Drawing from machine learning systems research, we examine how data and concept drift can persist undetected as models continue producing outputs despite shifting feature distributions. The risk intensifies in chained pipelines, where probabilistic errors compound across stages—for example, when upstream model inaccuracies propagate and amplify downstream.

We also analyze non-deterministic inference behavior, which complicates reproducibility, auditability, and root cause analysis in cloud environments. The session highlights risks of AI-generated data contamination, where synthetic outputs are mistakenly treated as ground truth, accelerating feedback loops and long-term model degradation.

At the infrastructure level, we discuss challenges such as non-linear inference cost scaling, observability gaps that mask semantic failures, and automation complacency that reduces human oversight.

The talk concludes with practical design principles for cloud data systems, including metadata-first architectures, explicit trust boundaries, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints to build resilient, auditable, and trustworthy AI-driven pipelines.

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: SMR Mayapple Trail Runs
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Runners are invited to register for the 17th annual Mayapple Trail Runs to be held on Saturday, May 16, 2026 in beautiful South Mountain Reservation in Essex County, N.J. based out of the Locust Grove Picnic Area, 197 Glen Avenue, Millburn.

The races are organized by the volunteers of the South Mountain Conservancy (SMC) with the support of the Essex County Parks Department.
The USA Track and Field sanctioned races provide an informal but supportive trail race environment for runners who are up to the challenge of completing 5, 10, or 20 miles, or ultra-marathoon distances of 50K (31 miles) and 50 miles. There is also an informal, untimed one-mile race for kids 14 and under. Starting times depend on the race. The 50k and 50 mile races start at 8:30. All proceeds support the mission of the Conservancy as a steward and advocate for the park and its public programs.

## Millburn, NJ • 50 Miler, 50K, 20 Miler, 10 Miler, 5 Miler, Kids 1 Mile Run

**Registration closes: Sat, May 16 @ 11:59PM ET**
**To register as a runner or volunteer (we need you!) go to [Ultrasignup.](https://ultrasignup.com/register.aspx?did=135418) For very detailed descriptions and information on group discounts, go to our MTR [website](https://sites.google.com/view/south-mtn-mayapple-trail-runs).**

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

We occasionally use photos of events in promotional materials and social media. By attending the event, I consent to photos of me being used in promotional materials or on social media.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: SQL Server 2025: The Future of Data Starts Here
description:
Cloud Data Driven
SQL Server 2025 is more than just an upgrade—it’s a leap forward. From becoming an AI-ready database to delivering modern development tools, this release is packed with innovations that redefine performance, scalability, and productivity.

In this session, you’ll see demos and practical examples showcasing a wide range of enhancements, including:

Improved JSON handling
T‑SQL REST API integration
Expanded T‑SQL functionality, including fuzzy string matching and regular expression support
Time‑bound Extended Events
Change event streaming for real‑time data integration
Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric
Updates to change tracking and columnstore indexes

Whether you’re a developer, DBA, or architect, this session will help you understand where SQL Server 2025 fits into your broader data platform strategy. Join us to explore what’s new and improved in SQL Server 2025!

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available courtesy of our host! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
*
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.

title: Centralized Data Lakes in the Cloud: Scalable and Governed Architectures
description:
Cloud Data Driven
Global data creation is projected to reach 175 zettabytes, with enterprises managing the majority of this growth. In data-intensive industries, organizations process an average of 9.1 petabytes per enterprise, exposing the limitations of traditional data warehouses. Yet 76% of organizations struggle with data lake governance, and 68% lack clear data ownership.

This session presents a research-backed framework for building centralized, cloud-based data lakes that deliver measurable improvements in performance, cost, and governance. Mature implementations achieve 3.2× faster time-to-insight, 54% reduction in data preparation effort, and 59.3% lower storage costs. Multi-layered architectures improve query performance by 73%, increase resource utilization by 81%, and reduce bottlenecks by 67.4%.

Cloud-native distributed storage supports 3.2× higher performance for mixed workloads, enables 8.7× more concurrent users, and delivers 99.99% availability. Beyond architecture, strong governance and master data management reduce inconsistencies by 83.6%, increase data utilization by 412%, and improve decision-making speed by 47.2%.

Security-by-design approaches reduce unauthorized access by 78.4%, lower breach likelihood by 56.2%, and decrease compliance penalties by 85.4%.

Attendees will gain practical, data-driven strategies to design scalable, secure, and governance-centric cloud data lakes.

title: Help Desk - Bring Your Laptop, Yourself, and Your WordPress Problems
description:
WordPress Montclair Meetup
If you are running a WordPress site and are having issues and would like some help, then this Meetup is for you!

If you know some things, expert or not, and want to give back to the WordPress community, then this Meetup is for you too!

We will do our best to match a problem with a problem-solver. On-site and in real-time. Just bring yourself, your laptop, and your WordPress problems.

It's OK if you attend and only want to observe. We recognize this is how people learn. Given our time limit of two hours, we may not be able to solve everyone's issues, but we will try! If you are seeking help, please come prepared. Have your web site loaded in a browser, have your WordPress admin ready, along with a short list of issues you are trying to solve.

People helping are volunteers and may not be experts. There is risk to breaking your site! Please do not bring client or business sites that can impact reputations or revenue. If you have a staging site we can test things with, that is always preferred. If it is a production site, please create a site backup before having someone do any work on your site.

If you cannot find the group, please ask the front desk where the WordPress meetup group is meeting.

**PARKING**:
We've been asked to not park in the United Way lot behind the library as it could result in a parking ticket. Please find street parking or a parking deck.

\-\-\-
Join the **JerseyPress Slack** group to chat with others in our community and get help: [https://jerseypress.org/slack/](https://jerseypress.org/slack/)

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Midday MeetUp - Bonnie Rapp, Best of Broadway
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Bonnie Rapp, Best of Broadway   
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Experience *The Best of Broadway* as Bonnie Rapp brings to life unforgettable moments from Tony Award–winning musicals. Relive the golden age of musical theatre through beloved classics and timeless melodies by legendary composers such as Rodgers and Hammerstein, Lerner and Loewe, Frank Loesser, Irving Berlin and more!  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e6f2368f110026c0c486  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
 About Bonnie Rapp**  
Bonnie had the honor of performing the lead role of Christine Daaé in the National Tour/San Francisco Company of Andrew Lloyd Webber's The Phantom of the Opera. Some of Bonnie’s favorite roles include Maria in The Sound of Music, Kate in Kiss Me, Kate, Cinderella in Rodgers and Hammerstein’s Cinderella, Louise in Gypsy and Sister Amnesia and Sister Leo in Nunsense, Nunsense II and Nunsense Jamboree. In addition, Bonnie has appeared in numerous galas, fundraisers, and Broadway concerts, including performances at Lincoln Center's Avery Fisher Hall.  
  
For the past decade, Bonnie has devoted her talents to providing live musical entertainment for a wide range of audiences, including corporate and community events, libraries, private celebrations, JCCs, and residents of independent living, assisted living, nursing homes, and memory care communities.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

description:
Green Team/Creation Justice Ministry monthly meetings: 2nd Sunday of the month, after worship, in the FCC library.  Our congregation is committed to the idea that creation care is central to each aspect of our church: our theology and worship, institutional life and practices, and our partnership and work in the wider community. Learn about FCC’s climate justice initiatives, be part of planning our events, and live your faith by taking action for the planet. 
Check out our Green Team Ministry page here: https://fccmontclair.org/green-team-ministry/
and read our Creation Justice Covenant here: https://www.fccmontclair.org/creation-justice-church/
Contact Green Team at greenteam@fccmtc.com.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

Regular rehearsals are on Wednesday evenings from 7:30 to 9:00 PM in the Sanctuary. For more information contact Markus.

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Underground Church Worship Service,10:30am on the Labyrinth in the Sanctuary
description: Underground Church (usually every 3rd Saturday) is a unique and dynamic gathering that meets monthly, and combines the intimacy of Jesus' early community with creative and inventive worship experiences.  In the warmth and safety of community we explore the scriptures, live out the questions through discussion,  and share innovative rituals that bring us closer to each other and to our still-speaking God. Together we create the unity of spirit that helps us find God's presence in the messy and beautiful experience of living today.  All are welcome.

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

We occasionally use photos of events in promotional materials and social media. By attending the event, I consent to photos of me being used in promotional materials or on social media.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available courtesy of our host! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.
* **Please engage with others in good faith, sportsmanship, respect and common sense.** *ABSOLUTELY NO harassment, getting physical, hurtful insulting, and persecutory language will be tolerated.* Repeat violators of venue rules AND/OR this rule may be asked to leave the event and not return.
source: Meetup: Board Game Night at Verona Inn

title: Shabbat Your Way
description:
Whether in your own home or someone else’s, with one person or many, having an elaborate meal or ordering take-out, there is not one way to celebrate Shabbat. “Shabbat Your Way” is an opportunity to cultivate a Shabbat mindset in a way that feels right for you. Shomrei will provide resources and ideas to help you add something new or special to your Shabbat practice or gathering.  
  
  
**Season Schedule**  
https://shomrei.org/calendar/shabbat-your-way-calendar

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Writing as Spiritual Practice Covenant Group
description:
Are you interested in exploring the monthly themes through writing and sharing with others? The purpose of this group is to write about the monthly theme before the meeting and share your writing with the group. Facilitated by Dennis Kurtti, it will be held each month on the fourth Saturday. This is not a writing critique group, and all levels of writing experience are welcome. If you would like to understand more about this kind of covenant group, you can watch the worship service held on 8/21/22, which featured speakers from an already existing group. To express your interest in this group, contact Laura Wilson at CovenantGroups@uumontclair.org

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

We occasionally use photos of events in promotional materials and social media. By attending the event, I consent to photos of me being used in promotional materials or on social media.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Help Desk - Bring Your Laptop, Yourself, and Your WordPress Problems
description:
WordPress Montclair Meetup
If you are running a WordPress site and are having issues and would like some help, then this Meetup is for you!

If you know some things, expert or not, and want to give back to the WordPress community, then this Meetup is for you too!

We will do our best to match a problem with a problem-solver. On-site and in real-time. Just bring yourself, your laptop, and your WordPress problems.

It's OK if you attend and only want to observe. We recognize this is how people learn. Given our time limit of two hours, we may not be able to solve everyone's issues, but we will try! If you are seeking help, please come prepared. Have your web site loaded in a browser, have your WordPress admin ready, along with a short list of issues you are trying to solve.

People helping are volunteers and may not be experts. There is risk to breaking your site! Please do not bring client or business sites that can impact reputations or revenue. If you have a staging site we can test things with, that is always preferred. If it is a production site, please create a site backup before having someone do any work on your site.

If you cannot find the group, please ask the front desk where the WordPress meetup group is meeting.

**PARKING**:
We've been asked to not park in the United Way lot behind the library as it could result in a parking ticket. Please find street parking or a parking deck.

\-\-\-
Join the **JerseyPress Slack** group to chat with others in our community and get help: [https://jerseypress.org/slack/](https://jerseypress.org/slack/)

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available courtesy of our host! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.
* **Please engage with others in good faith, sportsmanship, respect and common sense.** *ABSOLUTELY NO harassment, getting physical, hurtful insulting, and persecutory language will be tolerated.* Repeat violators of venue rules AND/OR this rule may be asked to leave the event and not return.
source: Meetup: Board Game Night at Verona Inn

title: WordPress Hangout & Networking
description:
WordPress Montclair Meetup
Come to network, chat, talk shop, or just hang out with like-minded WordPress folks. Alcoholic\* and non-alcoholic beverages, along with a variety of food options will be available to purchase. Hope to see you there!

We will be meeting at Just Jake's in the backroom area.

\*Please be safe and no drinking to excess.

\-\-\-
Join the **JerseyPress Slack** group to chat with others in our community and get help: [https://jerseypress.org/slack/](https://jerseypress.org/slack/)
